pqc_capability_description: First quantum-safe fork of Bitcoin. Testnet replaces ECDSA with NIST-standardized ML-DSA signatures to protect against quantum threats. Increased block size to 64 MiB to accommodate larger PQC signatures (38-72x larger than ECDSA). Full transaction lifecycle with quantum-resistant cryptography. Protects ~6.26M BTC in addresses with exposed public keys against Harvest Now Decrypt Later attacks.
product_brief: First quantum-safe Bitcoin fork demonstrating ML-DSA feasibility for blockchain signatures. Open permissionless testnet for miners developers researchers and users.

pqc_capability_description: Japan's first remote access system with built-in post-quantum cryptographic protection powered by 01 Quantum. Implements NIST FIPS 203 (ML-KEM) to protect against Harvest Now Decrypt Later attacks. Free upgrade for existing customers. Serving ~7000 companies and 36000 users primarily in finance sector. In production since 2002.
fips_validated: No (implements FIPS 203 algorithm; no NIST CMVP validation)
product_brief: First commercial remote access product in Japan with production-ready NIST-standardized PQC. Demonstrates enterprise readiness for PQC in remote access scenarios.

pqc_capability_description: First post-quantum testnet for Solana blockchain developed in collaboration with Solana Foundation. Replaces EdDSA signatures with NIST-standardized ML-DSA demonstrating quantum-resistant transactions are practical on high-throughput blockchain architectures (65000+ TPS). Open-sourced testnet with readiness assessments and migration test environments. $20M Series A funding announced January 2026.
product_brief: Demonstrates high-throughput blockchains can successfully implement post-quantum signatures. Critical for Web3 and decentralized finance PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: Post-quantum semiconductor-based trust anchors deployed in U.S. systems. QS7001 and QVault-TPM post-quantum secure processors incorporating quantum-resistant algorithms and hardware roots of trust. Protection against harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks. Millions of devices deployed worldwide. Joint venture with Kaynes SemiCon in India for PQC chip manufacturing.
product_brief: Post-quantum semiconductor chips with embedded quantum-resistant cryptography. First deployments in U.S. government and critical infrastructure.

pqc_capability_description: Hardware-based plug-and-play post-quantum encryption platform. NSA CNSA 2.0 compliant protocol-agnostic solution working with legacy and modern networks. Features one-way data diode with NIST-approved PQC algorithms including ML-KEM. Tested by U.S. military branches with SpaceX deployment. Secures data in transit without software rewrites or PKI dependencies.
product_brief: Hardware-based quantum-resistant encryption for high-security environments. Deployed across U.S. military branches and SpaceX missions.

pqc_capability_description: First end-to-end cryptographic security platform for quantum era. Provides cryptographic discovery (Recon) crypto-agility engine (Resilience) and real-time compliance reporting. Enables dynamic cipher key and algorithm updates without code changes or downtime. Works across cloud on-premise and edge environments. Includes QuEverywhere for mobile PQC.
product_brief: Platform enabling organizations to transition to PQC with crypto-agility and zero-downtime cipher updates.

pqc_capability_description: Platform for automated discovery and inventory of cryptographic assets. Facilitates transition to post-quantum cryptography and enhances cyber readiness against AI-powered attacks. Provides continuous visibility into cryptographic dependencies. Deployed in U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) CIO systems. HQC algorithm contributor selected by NIST.
product_brief: Crypto-agility platform with automated cryptographic asset discovery for PQC migration. Powers DoD quantum readiness.

pqc_capability_description: NIST-aligned quantum-safe cryptographic engine. Powers Hitachi DoMobile Ver.5 remote access system in Japan. Developing Quantum AI Wrapper (QAW) for securing AI data with Full Homomorphic Encryption and Quantum Crypto Wrapper (QCW) for blockchain quantum-safety. Early-stage quantum-safe security leader.
product_brief: NIST-compliant quantum-safe engine enabling partners to deploy PQC. Focus on agile deployment and AI/blockchain security.

pqc_capability_description: Implements HQC (Hamming Quasi-Cyclic) and ML-KEM for .NET platform. Provides quantum-resistant key exchange mechanisms with good performance characteristics on .NET runtime.
product_brief: C# implementation of cryptographic algorithms for .NET platform with PQC interoperability support.

pqc_capability_description: Comprehensive PQC implementation supporting ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and HQC (Hamming Quasi-Cyclic). Provides quantum-resistant key encapsulation and digital signatures through JCE/JCA provider. Supports hybrid classical/PQC modes for gradual migration.
product_brief: Comprehensive Java implementation of cryptographic algorithms. Supports JCE/JCA provider lightweight API PGP S/MIME DTLS TLS OCSP TSP CMP and certificate generation.

pqc_capability_description: Long-term support release with ML-DSA composite signatures, enabling hybrid quantum-classical signature schemes. Allows organizations to use both traditional (RSA/ECDSA) and post-quantum signatures simultaneously for defense-in-depth.
product_brief: Long-term support release with enterprise stability. Includes PQC support and supply chain transparency features.

pqc_capability_description: Full support for NIST-standardized PQC algorithms including ML-KEM (Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism) for key exchange, ML-DSA (Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm) for signatures, and SLH-DSA (Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Algorithm). Enables quantum-safe TLS 1.3 connections with hybrid mode support.
product_brief: Robust commercial-grade TLS/SSL and crypto library. Implements TLS 1.3 DTLS 1.3 and QUIC protocols. Reference implementation for cryptographic operations used by majority of HTTPS websites.

pqc_capability_description: Multi-language library (C++, Go, Java). PQC algorithm integration in progress but not yet shipped in stable releases. Used in Google infrastructure and Cloud KMS.
product_brief: Google's multi-language crypto library designed for safe and simple API usage. PQC suite integration in progress.

pqc_capability_description: C++ cryptography library with extensive PQC support recommended by BSI. Includes Classic McEliece, FrodoKEM, and all NIST-standardized algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA). Botan 2.x reached end-of-life on 2025-01-01.
product_brief: Feature-rich C++ crypto library. BSI project for PQC reference implementations.

pqc_capability_description: FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules. Supports hybrid mode (classical + PQC) and includes CryptoComply PQ TLS for quantum-safe data in transit. Drop-in OpenSSL compatibility. CryptoComply Go v4.0 adds all NIST PQC algorithms (ML-KEM FIPS 203 ML-DSA FIPS 204 SLH-DSA FIPS 205). PQC capabilities rolled out across Core v3.5 Mobile v3.5 Java v4 and Go v4.
product_brief: Enterprise cryptographic modules with immediate PQC support and FIPS validation.

pqc_capability_description: Version 8.6 introduces quantum-safe TLS 1.3 with hybrid key exchange (X25519MLKEM768). Integrates liboqs implementations of ML-KEM and ML-DSA.
product_brief: SAP's proprietary cryptographic library now enabling PQC for SAP applications and secure communications.

pqc_capability_description: Comprehensive SDK for PQC integration. Features PQCryptoLib-Core (FIPS 140-3 certified) and PQCryptoLib-Soft. Supports mobile, server, and embedded.
product_brief: High-assurance post-quantum cryptography SDK for enterprise and embedded security.

pqc_capability_description: Native ML-KEM-768 and ML-KEM-1024 since Go 1.24 (Feb 2025). Hybrid post-quantum TLS enabled by default since Go 1.24 combining classical elliptic-curve cryptography with ML-KEM. Go 1.26 adds crypto/hpke and crypto/mlkem/mlkemtest packages with derandomized encapsulation for known-answer tests. FIPS 203 compliant.
product_brief: Standard library implementation of NIST FIPS 203 key encapsulation. Hybrid PQC TLS default since Go 1.24; Go 1.26 adds crypto/hpke and mlkemtest.

pqc_capability_description: Pure Rust implementation of NIST FIPS 203 ML-KEM. Modular, zero-unsafe code (mostly), and part of the RustCrypto organization.
product_brief: Pure Rust ML-KEM implementation standardizing on FIPS 203.

pqc_capability_description: Pure Rust implementation of NIST FIPS 204 ML-DSA signatures. Provides highly modular and type-safe interface for next-gen digital signatures.
product_brief: Pure Rust ML-DSA implementation compliant with FIPS 204.

pqc_capability_description: Pure Rust implementation of NIST FIPS 205 SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+) hash-based signatures. suitable for high-security applications requiring conservative security assumptions.
product_brief: Pure Rust SLH-DSA implementation compliant with FIPS 205.

pqc_capability_description: Rust bindings to PQClean C implementations. Access to a wide range of NIST PQC candidates and standardized algorithms.
product_brief: Rust wrapper around PQClean providing FFI bindings to reference implementations.

pqc_support: Yes (ML-KEM; ML-DSA; SLH-DSA; FrodoKEM; HQC; BIKE; Classic McEliece; FN-DSA; NTRU)

pqc_capability_description: Formally verified and optimized C/Assembly implementation of ML-KEM. Part of Linux Foundation PQCA PQ Code Package. Proven memory safety and functional correctness. Reached stable v1.0.0 release.
product_brief: High-assurance production-ready ML-KEM implementation formally verified for correctness.

pqc_support: Yes (ML-KEM ML-DSA HQC SHA-3 SHAKE Ascon)

product_brief: Lean crypto library optimized for small footprint embedded environments.

pqc_capability_description: Implementation of ETSI TS 104 015 Quantum-Safe Hybrid Key Exchanges with Hidden Access Policies. Enables attribute-based encryption (ABE) with post-quantum security.

pqc_capability_description: Library for benchmarking and testing PQC on ARM Cortex-M4 microcontrollers. Contains optimized implementations of ML-KEM, ML-DSA, etc.

product_brief: Reference implementation of the Hamming Quasi-Cyclic (HQC) KEM.

pqc_capability_description: Rust library for Citadel Protocol with multi-layered encryption and anti-replay protection. Features novel 3D matrix ratcheting and scramcryption.
product_brief: PQC library for Citadel Protocol with built-in anti-replay and ratcheting.

pqc_capability_description: Core cryptographic library for Windows Azure and Microsoft 365. Now integrated with ML-KEM ML-DSA and XMSS stateful signatures. Undergoing FIPS 140-3 validation for PQC module.
product_brief: Microsoft's core crypto library powering Windows Azure and O365. Optimized for performance and FIPS compliance.

pqc_capability_description: AWS's open-source crypto library derived from BoringSSL. FIPS 140-3 validated with ML-KEM and ML-DSA support. Powers AWS KMS, Secrets Manager, and Certificate Manager. Hybrid PQ KeyShares enabled by default in v1.68.0.
product_brief: FIPS 140-3 validated crypto library for AWS services. Includes optimized ML-KEM and ML-DSA. Hybrid PQ KeyShares enabled by default.

pqc_capability_description: GPU-accelerated PQC library for high-throughput workloads. Achieves 143x/99x/84x speedup over CPU for ML-KEM Keygen/Encapsulation/Decapsulation. product_brief: GPU-accelerated PQC SDK. Delivers massive throughput for ML-KEM and ML-DSA on Nvidia hardware. Now integrated with OQS.

pqc_capability_description: IOS XE 26 delivers industry-first full-stack post-quantum cryptography for enterprise routing and switching. Protects networking devices data in transit and control plane traffic with NIST-approved algorithms. Supports 8000 Series Secure Routers and C9000 Series Smart Switches. Also maintains RFC 8784 PQC PSK for IKEv2/IPsec VPN protection.
product_brief: Full-stack quantum-safe networking OS with NIST PQC algorithms for routing switching and VPN.

pqc_capability_description: Application Layer Transport Security with hybrid post-quantum key exchange (NTRU-HRSS + X25519). Protections Google's internal infrastructure traffic against quantum decryption.

pqc_capability_description: iMessage protocol (PQ3) achieving Level 3 security with hybrid ML-KEM since iOS 17.4 (March 2024). CryptoKit framework in iOS 26/macOS 26 extends PQC with ML-KEM 768/1024 and ML-DSA-65/ML-DSA-87 APIs for third-party apps via HPKE. Apple services including CloudKit Push Notifications Safari Maps Weather and iCloud Private Relay adopting quantum-secure TLS.
primary_platforms: iOS macOS iPadOS watchOS tvOS visionOS
product_brief: First messaging protocol to achieve Level 3 security with separate PQC rekeying (iOS 17.4). System-wide PQC support in Apple ecosystem via CryptoKit ML-KEM and ML-DSA APIs (iOS 26).

pqc_capability_description: Full PQC suite in firmware v13.8.0+. ML-KEM (512/768/1024), ML-DSA (44/65/87), SLH-DSA (all 12 param sets) natively via PKCS#11. LMS/HSS and XMSS available via PQSDK C API. All CAVP validated Sep 2025. FIPS 140-3 Level 3 (cert #4765; PQC resubmission pending).
product_brief: Enterprise HSM with full NIST PQC suite. Native ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA (all 12 param sets) in firmware v13.8.0+, CAVP validated. LMS/XMSS via PQSDK. CodeSafe secure execution. CNG and JCE interfaces supported.

pqc_capability_description: Production-ready PQC with native ML-KEM (FIPS 203) ML-DSA (FIPS 204) and LMS/HSS (SP 800-208) support integrated into firmware v7.9+. Software v7.9.1 adds hybrid KEM (ML-KEM) for NTLS SSH and REST APIs. FW v7.9.2 adds Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) via REST API PQC key attestation and USB HSM (G7) PQC extension. FIPS 140-3 Level 3 validated; PQC-inclusive FIPS 140-3 certification in progress.
product_brief: Enterprise HSM with production-ready native PQC algorithms. FIPS 140-3 Level 3 validated with ML-KEM and ML-DSA.

pqc_capability_description: Full PQC support via Q-safe firmware extension (v5.0+). Supports ML-KEM-512/768/1024, ML-DSA-44/65/87, XMSS, and LMS. Highest physical security at FIPS 140-2 Level 4. Supports custom PQC algorithm loading through CryptoServer SDK. Available in network and PCIe form factors.
product_brief: General purpose HSM platform for enterprise cryptographic operations and key management.

pqc_capability_description: Experimental support for ML-DSA and SLH-DSA in Transit secrets engine. Enables quantum-resistant signing-as-a-service for protecting data in transit. Early adoption in enterprise secrets management.
product_brief: Enterprise secrets management encryption-as-a-service and privileged access management. Supports PKI SSH transit encryption.

pqc_capability_description: Full PQC key management with ML-KEM ML-DSA and SLH-DSA key types. Supports encapsulation decapsulation sign and verify operations. PQ-TLS for KMIP connections. nShield 5 HSM v13.8.0 natively supports PQC with CAVP validation.
product_brief: Enterprise key management with production PQC key types. Integrated with nShield 5 HSMs for hardware-accelerated quantum-safe key lifecycle.

pqc_capability_description: GKLM does not have PQC key types yet. IBM launched Guardium Cryptography Manager (Oct 2025) for PQC migration with posture visibility and remediation workflows. GKLM supports crypto-agility for future PQC adoption.

product_brief: Enterprise key lifecycle management with KMIP support. PQC migration via companion Guardium Cryptography Manager.

pqc_capability_description: No PQC features in v21.13. Oracle PQC efforts focused on database TLS (ML-KEM in Oracle 26ai) and JDK-level PQC (ML-DSA in JDK 26). Key Vault PQC expected via Oracle ecosystem integration.
product_brief: Oracle database encryption key management. PQC not yet available; expected through broader Oracle platform PQC rollout.

pqc_capability_description: PQC key management via u.trust Anchor HSM with Quantum Protect package. ML-KEM ML-DSA and LMS passed NIST CAVP testing (Oct 2025). First industry-certified KMIP v2.1 implementation.
product_brief: Enterprise key management with PQC-ready architecture. Integrated with u.trust Anchor HSM for quantum-safe key lifecycle.

pqc_capability_description: Commercial PKI platform with full PQC certificate lifecycle management. The EJBCA Hardware Appliance includes a FIPS 140-2 Level 3 certified HSM.

pqc_capability_description: AWS TLS implementation with ML-KEM hybrid key exchange. Kyber support removed in v1.7.0 in favor of standardized ML-KEM only. Used across AWS services to provide quantum-safe communications. Designed for high-performance cloud environments.
product_brief: AWS's C99 implementation of TLS/SSL protocols. Designed for simplicity security and performance. Kyber removed in v1.7.0; ML-KEM only.

pqc_capability_description: Google's fork with ML-KEM support. Used in Chrome browser and Google infrastructure. Pioneering deployment of quantum-safe key exchange at internet scale.

product_brief: OpenBSD fork of OpenSSL focused on code modernization and security. Includes libtls for simplified secure programming.

Widely deployed in automotive, industrial, and aerospace.

product_brief: Lightweight portable TLS/SSL library for embedded devices to cloud. Supports TLS 1.3 DTLS 1.3 with small footprint. Includes wolfCrypt crypto engine.

product_brief: Open source signing platform with production ML-DSA and SLH-DSA support via Bouncy Castle. HSM integration with Fortanix DSM for PQC key storage.

pqc_capability_description: IPsec VPN with experimental ML-KEM support. Enables quantum-safe VPN tunnels for site-to-site and remote access scenarios. Active development for production deployment.

product_brief: Premier connectivity tool for SSH protocol. Provides secure tunneling multiple authentication methods. Default PQC key exchange since v10.0 (Apr 2025).

fips_validated: Yes (FIPS 140-2 L1 software / L3 Cloud HSM)
product_brief: Leading cloud KMS with production-ready PQC key types. Google enables customers to create and use ML-KEM and ML-DSA keys directly for application-level quantum-safe operations.

pqc_capability_description: ML-DSA digital signatures (FIPS 204) GA since June 2025 with ML-DSA-44/65/87 key specs via CreateKey/Sign/Verify APIs in FIPS 140-3 L3 HSMs. ML-KEM hybrid TLS for API transit protection via AWS-LC. PQC key import (BYOK) not supported — ML-DSA keys must be generated in KMS. External Key Store (XKS) symmetric-only — no PQC HYOK. AWS Private CA supports ML-DSA certificate issuance for code signing workflows.
product_brief: Managed cloud KMS with GA ML-DSA signing (FIPS 204) in FIPS 140-3 L3 HSMs. ML-KEM hybrid TLS for transit. No PQC BYOK/HYOK. AWS Private CA enables ML-DSA code signing.

pqc_capability_description: Microsoft cloud key management and secrets service. Underlying SymCrypt library supports ML-KEM and ML-DSA available via Windows CNG but not yet exposed as Azure Key Vault key types for customer operations. Managed HSM uses Marvell LiquidSecurity HSM. Microsoft targets quantum-safe key vault key types by 2029 per CNSA 2.0 compliance roadmap.
fips_validated: Yes (FIPS 140-2 L1 software / L3 Managed HSM)
product_brief: Cloud key management with HSM-backed storage. PQC in SymCrypt but not yet exposed as Key Vault key types; scheduled per Microsoft 2029 CNSA 2.0 quantum-safe roadmap.

pqc_support: Yes (ML-KEM ML-DSA LMS/HSS via Luna firmware)
pqc_capability_description: Luna HSM-as-a-Service via Thales Data Protection on Demand (DPoD). Luna Cloud HSM Services use same firmware as on-premises Luna HSMs — PQC available as firmware v7.9.x rolls out to cloud instances. ML-KEM (FIPS 203) ML-DSA (FIPS 204) and LMS/HSS (SP 800-208) supported. Available across AWS Azure and GCP marketplaces.
product_brief: Cloud HSM-as-a-Service via Thales DPoD with PQC via Luna firmware v7.9.x. ML-KEM ML-DSA and LMS/HSS. FIPS 140-2 L3 validated.

pqc_capability_description: Dedicated managed HSM service in AWS (FIPS 140-2 L3). ML-KEM-768 and ML-DSA-65 available through AWS Crypto Tools SDK integration. Native PKCS#11 PQC mechanisms not yet available in firmware. AWS KMS provides PQC TLS endpoints with ML-KEM hybrid. Managed service with multi-AZ redundancy.
product_brief: AWS dedicated cloud HSM. FIPS 140-2 L3 hardware. PQC firmware support on roadmap aligned with NIST standards.

pqc_support: Yes (Quantum entropy source for PQC; ML-KEM)
pqc_capability_description: Hardware QRNG based on photon detection providing certified quantum entropy. Available as PCIe card, USB device, and OEM chip. Generates true random numbers from quantum vacuum fluctuations at speeds up to 1 Gbps. Certified to AIS 31 and FIPS 140-2 standards. Provides quantum-grade entropy for PQC key generation, ML-KEM key pairs, and HSM seeding. ID Quantique acquired by IonQ in 2024.
product_brief: Market-leading hardware QRNG. Quantum entropy from photon detection for PQC key seeding HSM integration and high-assurance randomness. Supports FIPS 140-2 entropy source qualification.

pqc_support: Yes (Quantum entropy for PQC keys; ML-KEM)
pqc_capability_description: Cloud-delivered quantum entropy service using a quantum computer as the entropy source. Generates cryptographic keys with quantum-certified randomness via Quantinuum H-Series trapped-ion quantum computers. Keys seeded with quantum entropy and delivered via API for PQC key generation (ML-KEM keypairs). Integrates with Thales Luna HSM via Quantum Origin Platform. Available on Azure Marketplace.
product_brief: Quantum computer-sourced entropy service for PQC key generation. Integrates with HSMs and KMS for quantum-grade randomness seeding ML-KEM and classical key pairs.

pqc_capability_description: High-speed hardware QRNG generating quantum entropy via quantum optical noise at up to 1 Gbps. Available as PCIe and network-attached appliance. Integrates with QuintessenceLabs qCrypt key and policy management. Provides NIST SP 800-90B compliant entropy source suitable for PQC key generation and HSM seeding. Used in Australian government and financial sector deployments.
product_brief: High-speed hardware QRNG with quantum optical entropy. NIST SP 800-90B compliant. Used in government and finance for PQC key generation and HSM entropy seeding.

pqc_capability_description: Commercial QKD system for metropolitan and campus networks. Supports DV-QKD (Discrete Variable) using the COW (Coherent One-Way) and BB84 protocols. Integrates with ETSI QKD API standards for network interoperability. Supports hybrid QKD+PQC key generation for defense-in-depth quantum-safe key exchange. Deployed in Swiss banking (PostFinance), government networks, and data centers.
product_brief: Leading commercial QKD system with CV and DV modes. ETSI API compliant for network integration. Deployed in banking and government with hybrid QKD+PQC capability.

pqc_capability_description: Toshiba QKD system using differential phase shift (DPS) protocol achieving world-record key rates. Supports high-speed QKD over standard SMF up to 600 km via Twin Field QKD protocol (no trusted relay nodes required). Deployed in Tokyo QKD network and UK National Quantum Network. Toshiba Europe chairs ETSI ISG QKD standards committee. Integrates with PQC for hybrid quantum-safe key distribution in enterprise and government networks.
product_brief: High-speed QKD with world-record key rates. ETSI standards leader. Deployed in Tokyo and UK national quantum networks with PQC hybrid capability.

pqc_capability_description: QKD-as-a-service platform targeting subsea and long-distance fiber deployments. Uses Trusted Node architecture to extend QKD beyond single fiber spans. Provides quantum-safe key exchange integrated with PQC (ML-KEM) in hybrid mode for government and critical infrastructure. Focused on satellite-ground and metro QKD networks. Participation in European Quantum Internet Alliance (QIA) projects.
product_brief: QKD-as-a-service for long-distance and subsea networks. Hybrid QKD+PQC deployment model. Targeting government and critical infrastructure with trusted node architecture.

pqc_capability_description: Crypto-agile high-speed network encryptors supporting all four NIST PQC standards: ML-KEM (FIPS 203) ML-DSA (FIPS 204) SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) and ML-DSA (Falcon) via field-upgradeable firmware. Also supports XMSS and FrodoKEM. ETSI QKD interface for hybrid QKD+PQC deployments. FPGA-based crypto-agile design enables in-field firmware upgrades without hardware replacement. Covers RAN/O-RAN to edge datacenter and core networks including 5G environments. FIPS 140-2 Level 3 and Common Criteria EAL 4+ certified.
product_brief: Enterprise-grade crypto-agile network encryptors with production ML-KEM/ML-DSA/SLH-DSA/FN-DSA support. FPGA-based field-upgradeable. ETSI QKD interface. Used in NATO and government critical infrastructure.

pqc_capability_description: FPGA-based crypto-agile network encryptors supporting all NIST-selected PQC algorithms post August 2024 standardization. First-to-market with Quantum Resistant Encryption (QRE) supporting hybrid classical+PQC key exchange. Field-updateable without hardware replacement. Certified under Malaysia PKTN scheme (Confidential classification, September 2025). Supports both QKD and PQC hybrid deployments for critical infrastructure.
product_brief: First-to-market FPGA-based quantum-resistant network encryptors. Hybrid QKD+PQC support. PKTN certified for classified government use. Subsidiary of Thales.

pqc_capability_description: World-first BSI-approved 400G quantum-safe Layer 1 network encryptor (September 2025). Uses AES-256 with hybrid crypto-agile key exchange combining PQC and classical Diffie-Hellman. ConnectGuard™ technology provides ultra-low-latency encryption. Protects against Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later attacks. German Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) certified — most rigorous European network encryption approval.
product_brief: World-first BSI-approved 400G quantum-safe Layer 1 encryptor. ConnectGuard hybrid PQC+classical AES-256 for optical transport. Protects against HNDL attacks.

pqc_capability_description: Industry-leading 1.6 Tbps quantum-safe optical transport encryption. WaveLogic 6 Extreme integrates NIST PQC algorithms with AES-256-GCM in hybrid mode and supports QKD vendor interoperability for defense-in-depth quantum security. Protects long-distance DWDM optical networks at Layer 1. Existing WaveLogic 5e 800G deployments upgradeable via software.
product_brief: 1.6 Tbps quantum-safe optical transport with hybrid PQC+QKD. Industry-leading throughput for DWDM backbone networks. Software-upgradeable from 800G deployments.

pqc_capability_description: PKI software with PQC support via Bouncy Castle integration. Can issue X.509 certificates with quantum-resistant signatures (ML-DSA) and support hybrid certificate chains.
product_brief: Enterprise PKI and Certificate Authority software. Supports X.509 CMP SCEP EST ACME protocols.

pqc_capability_description: PQC pioneer with hybrid certificate issuance capabilities. Supports hybrid X.509 certificates combining classical RSA/ECDSA with ML-DSA signatures. PQC certificate testing environment available. Automated discovery of quantum-vulnerable certificates.
product_brief: Leading CA with PQC hybrid certificate capabilities. Automated certificate lifecycle management with quantum readiness assessment.

pqc_capability_description: Dedicated PQC readiness page indicates active development. Certificate lifecycle management platform preparing for PQC certificate issuance. Hybrid certificate support expected.
product_brief: Major CA preparing for PQC certificate transition. Certificate lifecycle management platform with PQC readiness planning.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise platform for cryptographic discovery and quantum-safe posture management. Scans source code binaries network traffic and IT assets to create a comprehensive cryptographic inventory (CBOM). Risk-based prioritization identifies quantum-vulnerable algorithms and provides remediation guidance aligned with NIST CNSA 2.0 timelines. Integrates with IBM Concert for compliance dashboards. Part of IBM Quantum Safe portfolio alongside Qiskit SDK.
product_brief: Enterprise cryptographic posture management platform. Automated CBOM generation quantum-vulnerable algorithm detection and risk-based PQC migration planning.

pqc_capability_description: Google Chrome supports ML-KEM for TLS 1.3 key exchanges to protect against Harvest Now Decrypt Later attacks. ML-KEM (FIPS 203) is enabled by default in Chrome 131+ for all platforms.
product_brief: Dominant web browser with default ML-KEM support.

pqc_capability_description: Microsoft Edge supports ML-KEM for TLS 1.3 key exchanges. Enabled by default in Edge 131+.
product_brief: Enterprise web browser with default ML-KEM support.

pqc_capability_description: Mozilla Firefox supports ML-KEM for TLS 1.3 key exchanges via NSS. Enabled by default in Firefox 132+ for all platforms. Supports X25519MLKEM768 hybrid.
product_brief: Open-source web browser with default ML-KEM support.

pqc_capability_description: Apple Safari supports ML-KEM for TLS 1.3 key exchanges via the OS-level CryptoKit. Supported on iOS 18+ and macOS 15+.
product_brief: Apple ecosystem web browser with PQC via OS integration.

pqc_capability_description: First HSM submitted for FIPS 140-3 Level 3 certification with all NIST PQC algorithms. Powered by the QASM cryptographic core natively supporting ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205), and LMS stateful hash-based signatures. QxHSM v4.4 (Dec 2024) added ML-DSA and full NIST PQC OID support. QxOS 5 firmware (June 2025) delivers next-generation quantum-safe HSM platform. FIPS 140-3 Level 3 certification submitted March 2025, currently in NIST MIP list. Available in desktop (QxBMC-1), 1U three-blade (QxBMC-3), and 4U twelve-blade (QxBMC-12) chassis. Engineered, manufactured, and assembled in Canada.
product_brief: PQC-capable HSM with FIPS 140-3 Level 3 certification pending (submitted March 2025). Supports all NIST PQC algorithms via the QASM cryptographic core. Canadian sovereign hardware designed for government and enterprise quantum-safe deployments.

pqc_capability_description: HSM-as-a-Service built on Swiss Primus HSM hardware delivering full post-quantum cryptography support. Supports ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205), plus stateful hash-based signatures HSS-LMS and XMSS. Includes CloudHSM SBX (Sandbox) for testing PQC migration strategies with dual HSM synchronization before production rollout. REST API access via Transaction Security Broker (TSB) middleware — language-agnostic integration without client libraries. Available in ECO tier for smaller workloads and Enterprise tier for production. Swiss infrastructure; same FIPS 140-2 Level 3 and CC EAL4+ validated hardware as Primus HSM.
product_brief: Swiss HSM-as-a-Service with comprehensive PQC algorithm support. CloudHSM SBX sandbox environment enables safe PQC migration testing before production. REST API via Transaction Security Broker.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise-grade Web Application Firewall and API security platform, now part of the Thales portfolio following the December 2023 acquisition. Protects web applications and APIs against OWASP Top 10 threats, DDoS, bot attacks, and business logic abuse. Supports hybrid cloud and on-premise deployments. PQC support (ML-KEM hybrid TLS key exchange for TLS 1.3) not yet announced; roadmap expected as RFC 8446 + ML-KEM hybrid draft adoption grows across enterprise TLS termination infrastructure. Relevant to PQC migration planning as WAF products are TLS termination points requiring quantum-safe key exchange upgrades.
product_brief: Enterprise WAF and API security platform under Thales portfolio (acquired Dec 2023). PQC roadmap pending as ML-KEM hybrid TLS adoption grows; TLS termination points require quantum-safe key exchange upgrades.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise code signing platform with experimental PQC support (v24.3+). ML-DSA (ML-DSA) and SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+) signing key algorithms supported for Certificate and Key Pair environments. Software-only key storage (HSM support not yet available for PQC keys). Supported through CodeSign Protect clients libhsm API and PKCS#11. Part of CyberArk/Venafi broader PQC readiness initiative.
product_brief: Enterprise code signing platform with experimental ML-DSA and SLH-DSA support. Software-only PQC key storage via PKCS#11. Part of CyberArk/Venafi ecosystem.

pqc_capability_description: RPM 6.0 in RHEL 10.1 (Oct 2025) supports hybrid ML-DSA-87-Ed448 package signing via Sequoia-PGP with OpenPGP v6. Red Hat is the first major Linux distribution to sign all RPM packages with a hybrid PQC key. Multiple signatures per package supported. All Red Hat products released after October 2025 are signed with the PQC-safe key.
product_brief: First major Linux distro with production hybrid PQC package signing. ML-DSA-87-Ed448 via Sequoia-PGP/OpenPGP v6 in RPM 6.0.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise code signing platform with production ML-DSA support at highest CNSA 2.0 security level. Client-side hashing architecture for high performance. Integrates with Thales Luna HSMs Entrust nShield HashiCorp Vault AWS KMS AWS CloudHSM Google Cloud KMS and Azure Key Vault for key storage. Joint solution brief with Thales for PQC CNSA 2.0 code signing systems. Available on Azure Marketplace as SaaS.
product_brief: Enterprise PQC code signing platform with CNSA 2.0 ML-DSA support. Multi-HSM/KMS integration. Client-side hashing for performance. Thales partnership for PQC code signing.

pqc_capability_description: JDK 24 (March 2025) introduced ML-KEM and ML-DSA as JCA providers via JEP 496/497 but jarsigner PQC support is targeted for JDK 26 (March 2026). JAR signing with ML-DSA will be the first PQC code signing in the Java ecosystem. Bouncy Castle JCE provider can be used today as an alternative for Java PQC signing. Hybrid Public Key Encryption API also planned for JDK 26.
product_brief: Java code signing tool. ML-DSA available in JDK 24 JCA; jarsigner PQC integration targeted for JDK 26 (March 2026). Critical for Java ecosystem PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: ARM TrustedFirmware TLS library widely used in embedded IoT devices. LMS hash-based signatures available for verification. ML-KEM listed as Future on roadmap. ML-DSA prototype targeted Q1 2026 with initial support Q2 2026. TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 LTS targeted Q1 2026. Critical gap for IoT/embedded PQC migration as millions of devices depend on Mbed TLS.
primary_platforms: ARM Cortex-M Cortex-A Embedded IoT Linux Windows
product_brief: ARM TLS library for embedded/IoT. LMS only; ML-KEM/ML-DSA on roadmap for Q2 2026. Critical PQC gap for IoT migration planning.

pqc_capability_description: Mozilla Network Security Services library powering Firefox Thunderbird RHEL and SUSE crypto stacks. ML-KEM-768 production (X25519MLKEM768 hybrid) enabled by default in Firefox 132+ Desktop and Firefox 145+ Android. ML-DSA key generation signing and verification via PKCS#11 (NSS 3.116-3.118). Formally verified ML-KEM implementation by Cryspen. CIQ achieved CAVP certification for NSS PQC module (February 2026). FIPS 140-3 validation in progress.
product_brief: Mozilla crypto library with production ML-KEM and ML-DSA. Formally verified PQC (Cryspen). CAVP certified. Powers Firefox Thunderbird RHEL SUSE.

pqc_capability_description: .NET 10 (Nov 2025 GA) provides native PQC APIs: MLKem class (FIPS 203) MLDsa class (FIPS 204) SlhDsa class (FIPS 205) and CompositeMLDsa class for composite signatures. APIs marked [Experimental] under diagnostic SYSLIB5006 but are production-ready. Uses Windows CNG on Windows and OpenSSL 3.5+ on Linux. First-party Microsoft implementation with all three NIST PQC standards available to every .NET 10 application.
product_brief: Native .NET 10 PQC APIs with all three NIST standards (ML-KEM ML-DSA SLH-DSA) plus composite signatures. Uses CNG or OpenSSL backends.

pqc_capability_description: Government and defense HSM with v7.15.0 delivering first fully standardized ML-KEM (FIPS 203) and ML-DSA (FIPS 204) implementations. Also supports ML-DSA (Falcon pre-standard) and LMS/HSS (SP 800-208). Industry-first FIPS 140-compliant HSM with embedded Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) chip for quantum-enhanced key generation. Made in USA (design development manufacture support). CNSA 2.0 aligned. Designed for US Government intelligence defense and civilian agencies.
product_brief: Government/defense HSM with all NIST PQC standards and embedded QRNG. Made in USA. CNSA 2.0 aligned. First FIPS 140-compliant HSM with quantum random number generator.

pqc_capability_description: Sovereign post-quantum network encryptor announced November 2025 at European Cyber Week. Quantum-resistant algorithms aligned with ANSSI recommendations. Throughput up to 4x10 Gbps with very low latency. Common Criteria EAL4+ certification targeted. Centralized management. Designed for French and European public administrations operators of vital importance and defense industrial base. GA scheduled June 2026.
product_brief: Sovereign post-quantum network encryptor for EU classified communications. ANSSI-aligned. CC EAL4+ targeted. 4x10 Gbps. GA June 2026.

pqc_capability_description: Cloud key management for BYOK/HYOK across AWS Azure GCP and Google Workspace. Quantum-resilient TLS ciphers for Chrome/CCKM connections (GA v2.22.0+). ML-KEM-enabled channels for Google Workspace CSE BYOK key rotation. BYOK key rotation for AWS KMS supported since v2.19. SAP BYOK integration available. Released alongside CipherTrust Manager v2.22.1.
product_brief: Cloud BYOK/HYOK key manager with quantum-resilient TLS for Google Workspace CSE. Multi-cloud key rotation across AWS Azure GCP.

pqc_capability_description: Vault Enterprise 1.19+ transit secrets engine supports experimental PQC signing: ML-DSA-44/65/87 (FIPS 204) and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) with 12 parameter sets (SHA2/SHAKE 128/192/256 s/f variants). Hybrid signatures combining ML-DSA with ecdsa-p256 ecdsa-p384 ecdsa-p521 or ed25519. Marked experimental and not recommended for production workloads. HashiCorp plans staged PQC delivery across additional Vault capabilities.
product_brief: Enterprise secrets engine with experimental ML-DSA and SLH-DSA signing. Hybrid PQC+classical signatures. Transit engine for encryption-as-a-service.

pqc_capability_description: Windows 11 24H2 adds ML-KEM-768 and ML-DSA support to CNG (Cryptography API: Next Generation). Post-quantum key exchange available in TLS 1.3 via Schannel. Applications using CNG APIs gain PQC support without code changes. Available on all Windows 11 24H2 devices including desktops laptops and workstations.
fips_validated: Partial (CNG FIPS 140-3; PQC module under review)
product_brief: Windows 11 desktop OS with PQC support for ML-KEM and ML-DSA via the CNG framework and TLS 1.3 Schannel integration.

pqc_capability_description: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS ships with OpenSSL 3.0.x and provides liboqs and oqs-provider packages via official apt repositories enabling ML-KEM and ML-DSA algorithms. System TLS stack can be updated via OQS-Provider for OpenSSL without recompiling. Canonical roadmap includes native PQC in Ubuntu 24.10 and Ubuntu Pro FIPS module.
fips_validated: No (Ubuntu Pro FIPS available; PQC not FIPS-validated)
product_brief: Ubuntu LTS with PQC support via OpenSSL 3.x and liboqs apt packages. Most widely deployed Linux distribution for PQC migration testing.

pqc_support: Partial (OpenSSL OQS Provider; FIPS-validated PQC roadmapped RHEL 10)
pqc_capability_description: RHEL 9 ships with OpenSSL 3.x enabling ML-KEM and ML-DSA via the OpenSSL OQS provider as a technology preview. RHEL 9.5 adds experimental quantum-safe TLS key exchange. Full FIPS-validated PQC algorithms planned for RHEL 10. Red Hat Insights provides PQC readiness scanning for enterprise environments.
fips_validated: Partial (OpenSSL FIPS 140-3; PQC not in FIPS module)
product_brief: Enterprise Linux with NIST-standardized PQC via OpenSSL OQS provider. FIPS-validated PQC roadmapped for RHEL 10.

pqc_support: Yes (ML-KEM ML-DSA via OpenSSL 3.3+ and liboqs)
pqc_capability_description: Fedora 41 ships with OpenSSL 3.3+ and liboqs packages enabling ML-KEM-512/768/1024 and ML-DSA-44/65/87 algorithms. Serves as upstream integration test bed for RHEL PQC features. Fedora 40 change proposal introduced system-wide PQC crypto policy support. Applications linked against Fedora OpenSSL automatically gain PQC hybrid key exchange capability.
product_brief: Fedora Linux upstream integration platform for RHEL PQC features. Ships ML-KEM and ML-DSA via OpenSSL 3.3+ and liboqs packages.

pqc_capability_description: Debian 12 ships with OpenSSL 3.0.x and provides liboqs and oqs-provider via bookworm-backports enabling ML-KEM and ML-DSA. Debian 13 (Trixie) expected to ship with OpenSSL 3.3+ for native PQC. Base for Ubuntu and Raspberry Pi OS making Debian PQC adoption significant for embedded and server markets.
product_brief: Foundational Linux distribution with PQC support via liboqs backports. Base for Ubuntu and many enterprise distributions making Debian PQC adoption broadly impactful.

pqc_capability_description: Ericsson is actively contributing to NIST PQC IETF 3GPP and GSMA Post Quantum Task Network standards for quantum-safe mobile networks. PQC for 5G protocols (AKA SUCI TLS IKEv2) targeted in 3GPP Release 20/21. Crypto-agile approach for Radio System and 5G Core. 6G designed to be quantum-resistant from the start.
product_brief: Ericsson quantum-safe 5G platform. PQC for mobile network protocols targeted via 3GPP Rel-20/21. Active standards contributor to NIST IETF 3GPP GSMA.

pqc_capability_description: Nokia defense-in-depth approach combining PQC and QKD for quantum-safe 5G networks. NetGuard suite includes Certificate Manager and Identity Access Manager for telecom security. Quantum-safe optical transport via PSE-V encryption modules. Satellite QKD trial with Colt and Honeywell (announced Jun 2025; LEO QKD demo Q3 2026, production launch Q3 2027). Active 3GPP standards contributor.
product_brief: Nokia quantum-safe networking combining PQC QKD and symmetric key infrastructure. Defense-in-depth for 5G optical and enterprise networks. Satellite QKD trial with Colt and Honeywell targeting 2026-2027.

pqc_capability_description: Quantum-safe 5G SIM demonstrated with SK Telecom on commercial 5G standalone network. ML-KEM algorithm implemented natively in SIM operating system for quantum-resistant subscriber identity concealment (SUCI). Hybrid key exchange combining traditional asymmetric and PQC algorithms. Protects user SUPI from Harvest Now Decrypt Later attacks.
product_brief: Quantum-safe 5G SIM with ML-KEM subscriber identity encryption. Demonstrated on SK Telecom commercial 5G SA network. First PQC-protected SUCI implementation.

pqc_capability_description: Giesecke+Devrient global SIM market leader developing quantum-safe SIM and eSIM platform. Crypto-agility framework enables remote cryptographic updates for PQC migration. GSMA SGP.32 certified eSIM for 4G/5G IoT devices. Collaborating with BSI on quantum-resistant ID chips. First GSMA eUICC Security Assurance certified IoT product.
product_brief: Global SIM leader with crypto-agile architecture for PQC migration. GSMA SGP.32 certified eSIM. Collaborating with BSI on PQC-enabled ID chips.

pqc_capability_description: First mobile platform to ship PQC-based cloud data protection. Galaxy S25 series (One UI 7) integrates ML-KEM into Knox Matrix for quantum-safe Samsung Cloud backup restore and sync. Enhanced Data Protection (EDP) feature adds PQC layer to end-to-end encryption. S3SSE2A embedded Secure Element with dual PQC hardware accelerators.
product_brief: First mobile platform with PQC cloud data protection via Knox Matrix ML-KEM. S3SSE2A hardware-accelerated PQC Secure Element.

pqc_support: Yes (Keccak PQC Hardware Accelerator; ML-KEM; ML-DSA; SLH-DSA)
pqc_capability_description: First hardware accelerator designed for post-quantum cryptography using Keccak-based technology. Offloads ML-KEM (FIPS 203) ML-DSA (FIPS 204) and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) computations from main processor. Embedded in future IDEMIA quantum-safe chips for banking SIM automotive IoT and healthcare secure elements. Supports crypto-agility framework.
primary_platforms: Secure Element SIM Smart Card IoT
product_brief: Keccak-based PQC hardware accelerator for secure elements. Supports all NIST PQC algorithms. Embedded in future IDEMIA SIM banking and IoT chips.

pqc_capability_description: Global digital identity leader with crypto-agility framework for PQC migration. Quantum-safe ID chip proof-of-concept with Bundesdruckerei BSI and Infineon demonstrating PQC on national ID cards. PQC hardware accelerator for secure elements. Supporting eIDAS 2.0 EUDI Wallet ecosystem with quantum-resistant credential issuance roadmap.
product_brief: Global digital identity leader with PQC crypto-agility. Quantum-safe ID chip proof-of-concept with BSI. eIDAS 2.0 EUDI Wallet readiness.

pqc_capability_description: Modular open-source digital identity platform with ML-DSA support for verifiable credentials. Compliant with eIDAS 2.0 Swiss E-ID and ISO 18013-5 mDL standards. Supports W3C VCDM SD-JWT VC and OID4VC protocols. Delivered Lithuanian EU digital identity wallet sandbox.
product_brief: Open-source eIDAS 2.0 digital identity platform with ML-DSA verifiable credentials. Swiss E-ID and mDL compliant.

pqc_capability_description: Open-source identity infrastructure with hybrid PQC digital signature support (ML-DSA + ECDSA in parallel). Supports W3C Verifiable Credentials SD-JWT JSON-LD and multiple DID methods. Multi-language SDKs (Kotlin JVM JS) for credential issuance holding and verification. Presented hybrid PQC implementation at PKI Consortium PQC Conference.
product_brief: Open-source identity infrastructure with hybrid PQC (ML-DSA + ECDSA) for W3C Verifiable Credentials. Multi-language SDKs.

pqc_support: Planned (PQC via CNG/SymCrypt; CNSA 2.0 transition by 2029)
pqc_capability_description: Microsoft decentralized identity service for W3C Verifiable Credentials issuance and verification. PQC primitives (ML-KEM ML-DSA) available in underlying Windows CNG and SymCrypt but not yet exposed in Verified ID credential signing. Microsoft quantum-safe roadmap targets PQC transition by 2029 per CNSA 2.0.
product_brief: Microsoft decentralized identity for Verifiable Credentials. PQC via CNG/SymCrypt on roadmap. Entra PQC transition targeted by 2026.

pqc_capability_description: Digital identity platform building verifiable digital credentials with PQC-compatible cryptographic architecture. DHS SVIP Phase 1 awardee for privacy-enhancing technologies. Supports ISO 18013-5 mDL SD-JWT and W3C Verifiable Credentials standards. Crypto-agile design compatible with future PQC.
product_brief: DHS-backed digital identity platform with PQC-compatible credential architecture. mDL SD-JWT and W3C VC support.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise secure messaging platform migrated from Proteus to Messaging Layer Security (MLS) protocol. MLS designed to efficiently operate with ML-KEM for quantum-safe group key exchange. PQC via MLS expected as IETF standardizes PQC ciphersuites. On-premises and cloud deployment for regulated industries.
product_brief: Enterprise secure messaging with MLS protocol. PQC key exchange via MLS on roadmap. On-premises deployment for regulated industries.

pqc_capability_description: Decentralized secure messaging using Matrix protocol with Olm/Megolm end-to-end encryption via vodozemac (Rust) library. Current encryption uses X25519 and AES-256 with Double Ratchet. PQC not yet implemented as of February 2026. Used by French government (Tchap) German military (BwMessenger) and NATO.
primary_platforms: Cross-platform iOS Android Web Desktop Self-hosted
product_brief: Decentralized secure messaging used by French government and NATO. PQC planned for vodozemac crypto library. Self-hosted deployment option.

pqc_capability_description: Oracle AI Database 26ai Enterprise Edition with quantum-resistant TLS 1.3 connections. ML-KEM key exchange for database TLS. Hybrid mode combining ECDHE and ML-KEM for defense-in-depth. ML-DSA support for database authentication. First major database to ship production PQC TLS connectivity.
product_brief: First major database with production PQC TLS (ML-KEM hybrid). Quantum-resistant database connections in Oracle AI Database 26ai.

pqc_support: Planned (PQC TLS and Token Signing by 2026)
pqc_capability_description: Microsoft enterprise identity platform (formerly Azure Active Directory). PQC TLS transport available via Windows CNG ML-KEM in TLS 1.3 for authentication endpoints. OIDC/SAML token signing with ML-DSA on roadmap. Full PQC transition planned by 2029 per CNSA 2.0. Underpins identity for Microsoft 365 Azure and 1B+ users.
product_brief: Microsoft enterprise IAM (formerly Azure AD). PQC TLS transport and token signing on quantum-safe roadmap for 2026. Serves 1B+ identities.

pqc_capability_description: Europe's first ANSSI Common Criteria EAL6+ certified quantum-resistant smart card. Implements ML-DSA (FIPS 204) lattice-based digital signatures for quantum-safe identity protection. Designed for national ID cards health cards and driving licenses. Crypto-agile architecture supports classical and PQC algorithms simultaneously.
product_brief: Europe's first CC EAL6+ certified quantum-resistant smart card with ML-DSA for ID cards health cards and driving licenses.

pqc_capability_description: World's first Common Criteria EAL6 certified PQC implementation on a security controller. BSI-certified ML-KEM and ML-DSA on 28nm TEGRION platform with Integrity Guard 32 security architecture. Supports eSIM 5G SIM payment cards eHealth cards and personal ID applications. Cryptography library with standardized PQC algorithms.
primary_platforms: Security Controller Smart Card eSIM 5G SIM
product_brief: World's first CC EAL6 certified PQC security controller. ML-KEM + ML-DSA on 28nm TEGRION for smart cards eSIM and payment.

pqc_capability_description: FIPS 140-3 Level 3 certified Java Card 3.1 secure element platform. PQC firmware signature verification planned per CNSA 2.0 timeline. Supports contactless dual-interface and contact smart card applications. NXP collaborating with Keysight on ML-DSA side-channel countermeasure validation for embedded PQC.
primary_platforms: Smart Card Java Card Secure Element
product_brief: FIPS 140-3 L3 certified Java Card secure element. PQC firmware signatures planned. NXP validating ML-DSA side-channel countermeasures.

pqc_capability_description: Python's most widely used JWT library. No PQC algorithm support. Implements RS256, ES256, EdDSA, and HMAC signing via the Python cryptography package. PQC integration dependent on the cryptography library adding ML-DSA/ML-KEM support and IETF JOSE PQC standards finalization.
product_brief: Python JWT library. No PQC. Awaiting IETF JOSE PQC specification and Python cryptography library support.

pqc_capability_description: Linux IPsec/IKEv2 implementation with experimental PQC support via liboqs plugin. Supports hybrid IKEv2 key exchange using the RFC 9370 Additional Key Exchange framework with ML-KEM. Default IPsec stack in Red Hat Enterprise Linux, Fedora, and OpenWRT. Production PQC roadmap tied to draft-ietf-ipsecme-ikev2-mlkem standardization.
product_brief: Default IPsec stack for Red Hat Enterprise Linux and Fedora. Supports IKEv1/IKEv2 with experimental PQC via liboqs hybrid mode. Widely deployed in enterprise Linux and network gateway environments.

pqc_capability_description: Open-source software HSM implementing the PKCS#11 interface. Primary tool for testing and developing PKCS#11 applications without physical HSM hardware. Supports PQC via liboqs-provider for OpenSSL integration. Used for testing PKCS#11 PQC mechanism workflows before deploying to physical HSMs (Thales Luna, Entrust nShield). Maintained by OpenDNSSEC project.
product_brief: Open-source software PKCS#11 HSM for testing and development. Supports PQC via liboqs-provider. Enables PKCS#11 workflow testing without physical HSM hardware.

pqc_capability_description: Reference C implementation of LMS and HSS stateful hash-based signatures per RFC 8554, by David McGrew (Cisco) — co-author of RFC 8554. Supports all NIST SP 800-208 parameter sets. Full key generation, signing, and verification. Used as integration basis by multiple downstream projects including wolfSSL.
product_brief: The canonical LMS/HSS reference implementation from Cisco. Directly cited by NIST SP 800-208 and IETF RFC 8554. Essential starting point for organizations implementing stateful hash-based signatures for firmware or code signing.

pqc_capability_description: Official C reference implementation of XMSS and XMSS^MT per RFC 8391, maintained by the original XMSS authors. Covers all RFC 8391 parameter sets with SHA-2 and SHAKE hash functions. Includes key generation, signing, verification, and state persistence. Canonical source used to validate other XMSS implementations.
product_brief: The official XMSS and XMSS^MT reference implementation from the RFC 8391 authors. Covers all NIST SP 800-208 XMSS parameter sets. Essential reference for validating XMSS support or building compliant implementations.

pqc_capability_description: European Commission open-source reference implementation of the EUDI Wallet for Android and iOS. Implements eIDAS 2.0 EUDI Wallet specification with OpenID4VCI credential issuance and OpenID4VP credential presentation. Supports mso_mdoc (ISO 18013-5) and SD-JWT VC (RFC 9901) formats. PQC integration planned per EU ARF roadmap: ML-DSA replacing ECDSA by Dec 2030 for high-risk cases and Dec 2035 full transition. Current cryptography: ECDSA P-256.
product_brief: European Commission reference EUDI Wallet for Android and iOS. OpenID4VCI/VP protocol support for eIDAS 2.0 credential lifecycle. Reference implementation for all national EUDI Wallet deployments.

pqc_capability_description: TypeScript/JavaScript SSI SDK supporting OpenID4VCI OpenID4VP and DIF Presentation Exchange specifications. Implements W3C Verifiable Credentials SD-JWT VC and mDL (ISO 18013-5) credential formats. EUDI Wallet ARF compliant. Used in POTENTIAL Large-Scale Pilot (EU-funded 2023-2025). PQC integration planned as IETF JOSE/COSE PQC algorithm registrations finalize for ML-DSA credential signatures.
product_brief: Open-source TypeScript SSI SDK for EUDI Wallet-compliant credential issuance and presentation. OpenID4VCI/VP with SD-JWT VC and mDL. Used in EU POTENTIAL Large-Scale Pilot.

pqc_capability_description: Python-based SSI agent framework (Aries Cloud Agent Python) under Linux Foundation Hyperledger project. Supports OpenID4VCI OpenID4VP W3C Verifiable Credentials and AnonCreds credential formats. Active EUDI Wallet protocol development targeting eIDAS 2.0 compliance. PQC ML-DSA credential signatures planned as IETF JOSE/COSE PQC algorithm registrations finalize. Deployed in government digital identity programs across Canada Australia and EU.
product_brief: Leading open-source SSI agent framework (Hyperledger/Linux Foundation). OpenID4VCI/VP and AnonCreds support. Active EUDI Wallet protocol development. Deployed in government identity programs.

pqc_support: Planned (PQC via W3C VC PQC roadmap)
pqc_capability_description: Open-source enterprise digital credential management platform (AYANWORKS) supporting W3C Verifiable Credentials AnonCreds and OpenID4VCI issuance. Provides issuer holder and verifier services via REST API with multi-tenancy. Designed for government and enterprise digital identity programs with EUDI Wallet-compatible deployment. Modular architecture supports future PQC credential signature migration as W3C VC and IETF JOSE PQC standards mature.
product_brief: Enterprise open-source verifiable credential platform. W3C VCs AnonCreds and OpenID4VCI issuance via REST API. Multi-tenant. Government-focused digital identity deployments.

pqc_capability_description: EUDI Wallet-certified SDK and workflow automation platform by Animo Solutions. Supports OpenID4VCI OpenID4VP W3C Verifiable Credentials SD-JWT VC and ISO 18013-5 mDL credential formats. EUDI Wallet ARF conformant. Used in NOBID and EWC EU Large-Scale Pilots (2023-2025). PQC integration planned as EUDI ARF PQC requirements finalize following the Dec 2026 national roadmap deadline.
product_brief: EUDI-certified wallet SDK by Animo Solutions. OpenID4VCI/VP SD-JWT VC and mDL support. Used in NOBID and EWC EU Large-Scale Pilots.

pqc_capability_description: Azure Dedicated HSM uses Marvell LiquidSecurity HSM hardware (FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated). Microsoft has announced PQC algorithm support as part of its quantum readiness initiative targeting 2026. Supports PKCS#11, JCA/JCE, and CNG/KSP interfaces. Dedicated single-tenant hardware for highest compliance requirements.
product_brief: Azure single-tenant HSM service using Marvell LiquidSecurity hardware. FIPS 140-2 L3. PQC support on Microsoft quantum readiness roadmap.

pqc_capability_description: IBM Cloud HSM uses Utimaco SecurityServer hardware backend. FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated. IBM Quantum Safe roadmap includes HSM firmware updates for ML-KEM and ML-DSA support aligned with NIST standards. Part of IBM Quantum Safe portfolio.
product_brief: IBM Cloud managed HSM backed by Utimaco SecurityServer. FIPS 140-2 L3. PQC roadmap aligned with IBM Quantum Safe initiative.

pqc_capability_description: ST33G1M2 is a TCG TPM 2.0-compliant secure microcontroller (CC EAL4+ certified). Current firmware supports RSA-2048 and ECC P-256/P-384. STMicroelectronics has announced PQC algorithm roadmap via firmware upgrade for ML-DSA targeting 2026-2027. Used in laptops, servers, and embedded systems.
product_brief: TCG TPM 2.0 secure microcontroller. CC EAL4+. Widely deployed in laptop and server platforms. PQC firmware upgrade roadmap announced.

pqc_capability_description: Infineon OPTIGA TPM SLB 9672 implements TCG TPM 2.0 with RSA-2048 and ECC P-256/P-384. Infineon has publicly announced PQC algorithm support for future OPTIGA TPM generations targeting ML-DSA and ML-KEM. SLB 9672 is used in enterprise laptops and embedded platforms. CC EAL4+ certified.
product_brief: Enterprise TPM 2.0 chip from Infineon. CC EAL4+. Deployed in millions of laptops and servers. PQC migration requires next-generation hardware.

pqc_capability_description: Intel PTT is a firmware-based TPM 2.0 implementation built into Intel Core and Xeon processors. It uses the CPU security enclave rather than a discrete chip. Current TPM 2.0 operations are classical (RSA, ECC). PQC support would require firmware/microcode update from Intel. No public PQC roadmap as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Firmware TPM 2.0 integrated into Intel processors. Replaces discrete TPM chip. PQC requires firmware update; no public roadmap published.

pqc_capability_description: YubiHSM 2 is a compact USB-attached HSM supporting RSA-2048/4096, ECC P-256/P-384/P-521, and Ed25519. FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated. No PQC (ML-KEM/ML-DSA) support currently. Yubico has not published a PQC firmware roadmap for YubiHSM 2. PQC migration would require hardware replacement.
product_brief: Compact USB HSM for server-side cryptographic operations. FIPS 140-2 L3. No PQC support — replacement required for quantum-safe migration.

pqc_capability_description: OpenTitan is an open-source silicon root-of-trust project led by lowRISC and Google. Discrete chip (Earl Grey) implements secure boot, attestation, and key management. ML-DSA (FIPS 204) support for firmware signing is on the roadmap. Designed to replace proprietary silicon roots-of-trust with auditable open-source hardware.
product_brief: Open-source silicon root-of-trust. Auditable hardware replacing proprietary secure enclaves. ML-DSA planned for firmware signing.

pqc_capability_description: TianoCore EDK2 is the reference UEFI firmware implementation used by most x86 server and PC manufacturers. Secure Boot uses RSA-2048 + SHA-256 for firmware image signatures. PQC migration requires ML-DSA support in the UEFI signature database and PKCS#7 parser. Community work via OQS integration is in progress. Critical for post-quantum secure boot on all x86 platforms.
product_brief: Reference UEFI firmware implementation. Basis for Secure Boot on virtually all x86 platforms. PQC migration requires ML-DSA support for firmware signing.

pqc_capability_description: NXP SE050 is a plug-and-trust secure element providing ECC P-256/P-384/P-521, RSA-2048/4096, and AES-128/256. CommonCriteria EAL6+ certified. No PQC support. NXP SE051 (successor) includes ML-KEM and ML-DSA on its feature roadmap. Widely deployed in IoT, automotive, and payment systems.
product_brief: CC EAL6+ secure element for IoT and embedded use. PQC migration requires replacement with SE051 generation hardware.

pqc_capability_description: ARM TrustZone provides hardware-enforced isolation between secure and non-secure worlds on ARM Cortex-A/M processors. It is a platform architecture, not a crypto implementation. PQC support depends on the software running in the Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) — typically OP-TEE or proprietary TEE. No inherent PQC constraints but software must be updated.
product_brief: ARM hardware isolation architecture for Trusted Execution Environments. PQC support depends on TEE software (OP-TEE, proprietary). Platform upgrade required.

pqc_capability_description: Microchip ATECC608B is a CryptoAuthentication device providing ECC P-256, SHA-256, and AES-128. Widely deployed in IoT devices for device authentication and key storage. No PQC support and no public PQC roadmap. Migration requires hardware replacement. Used in AWS IoT, Azure IoT, and Google Cloud IoT integrations.
product_brief: ECC-only crypto co-processor for IoT device authentication. No PQC — hardware replacement required. Hundreds of millions deployed globally.

pqc_capability_description: Qualcomm Snapdragon SoCs include a Trusted Execution Environment (QTEE) based on ARM TrustZone plus a dedicated Secure Processing Unit (SPU). The SPU handles key storage and cryptographic operations for Android devices. Qualcomm has announced PQC algorithm support as part of its CNSA 2.0 roadmap for government-grade mobile security. ML-KEM and ML-DSA planned.
product_brief: Qualcomm Secure Processing Unit in Snapdragon SoCs. Handles mobile device key storage and crypto. PQC planned under CNSA 2.0 alignment.

pqc_capability_description: LUKS2 (Linux Unified Key Setup) uses AES-256-XTS for bulk disk encryption (quantum-safe) with Argon2id or PBKDF2 for passphrase key derivation. Key slots use classical symmetric wrapping. A PQC key exchange layer is not natively supported — TPM2 or hardware token integration uses classical protocols. Cryptsetup 2.7+ adds Token2 API; PQC key wrapping requires external tooling.
product_brief: Standard Linux disk encryption using dm-crypt kernel module. AES-256-XTS bulk is quantum-safe; passphrase/key-slot wrapping needs PQC extension.

pqc_capability_description: VxWorks is the world's most widely deployed RTOS, used in aerospace (Boeing 787, Mars rovers), defense systems, and industrial control. Wind River has announced PQC algorithm support via Wind River Studio for VxWorks 23.09+. Targets CNSA 2.0 compliance for defense and aerospace markets. Integration with Wind River Platform for Safety Certified environments.
product_brief: Industry-leading RTOS for safety-critical systems. Used in aerospace, defense, and industrial control. PQC support planned via Wind River Studio for CNSA 2.0 compliance.

pqc_capability_description: QNX Neutrino is a safety-certified microkernel RTOS used in automotive (50M+ vehicles), medical devices, and industrial systems. BlackBerry QNX has announced PQC integration aligned with NIST standards and ISO 21434 automotive cybersecurity requirements. Targets ML-KEM and ML-DSA for over-the-air update signing and secure communications.
product_brief: Safety-certified microkernel RTOS in 50M+ vehicles and critical systems. PQC roadmap targets automotive OTA update signing and secure communications per ISO 21434.

pqc_capability_description: FreeRTOS is an open-source RTOS for microcontrollers, maintained by Amazon. The corePKCS11 library provides cryptographic operations using classical algorithms (RSA, ECC, AES). No PQC support exists in any FreeRTOS component as of Q1 2026. The constrained memory profile of target microcontrollers (4KB-64KB RAM) makes ML-KEM/ML-DSA integration challenging. AWS has not published a PQC roadmap for FreeRTOS.
product_brief: Widely used open-source RTOS for microcontrollers. No PQC support — constrained memory profiles make ML-KEM/ML-DSA integration a significant challenge.

pqc_capability_description: Alpine Linux uses musl libc and is the default base image for millions of Docker containers. Alpine 3.20+ ships OpenSSL 3.3.x enabling ML-KEM via the liboqs-provider package. Alpine's minimal footprint (5MB base image) makes PQC library deployment size a consideration. Used in 5B+ Docker Hub image pulls. Container workloads using Alpine-based images can inherit PQC via OpenSSL provider.
product_brief: Minimal Linux for containers. Default base for billions of Docker images. PQC support via OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider package in Alpine 3.20+.

pqc_capability_description: FreeBSD uses OpenSSL as its base cryptographic library and ships OpenSSL 3.x in FreeBSD 14+. PQC support available via the security/liboqs and security/oqs-provider ports. The FreeBSD kernel crypto API does not natively support PQC algorithms. Used extensively in networking appliances (Netflix CDN, WhatsApp), embedded systems, and NAS devices.
product_brief: Production UNIX OS powering Netflix CDN and networking appliances. PQC available via liboqs ports on top of OpenSSL 3.x in FreeBSD 14+.

pqc_capability_description: openSUSE Leap 15.6+ ships OpenSSL 3.1.x with PQC provider support. SUSE's quantum-safe initiative includes crypto-policies integration for system-wide PQC configuration. openSUSE is the upstream for SUSE Linux Enterprise Server. liboqs-provider is available via the security repository.
product_brief: Community Linux distribution and SLES upstream. OpenSSL 3.x base enables PQC via provider mechanism. SUSE quantum-safe initiative drives roadmap.

pqc_capability_description: Rocky Linux 9+ ships OpenSSL 3.x with system-wide crypto-policies framework inherited from RHEL 9. The FUTURE crypto-policy enables stricter algorithms; PQC policy extension is in development aligned with Red Hat's roadmap. liboqs packages available via EPEL/community repositories. Rocky Linux mirrors RHEL's PQC timeline.
product_brief: RHEL-compatible enterprise Linux. OpenSSL 3.x + crypto-policies for system-wide PQC configuration. Mirrors Red Hat quantum-safe timeline.

pqc_capability_description: CentOS Stream 9/10 is the upstream development platform for RHEL, shipping OpenSSL 3.x with crypto-policies integration. Experimental PQC features appear in CentOS Stream before RHEL GA. ML-KEM and ML-DSA accessible via liboqs-provider. Acts as preview for RHEL quantum-safe features.
product_brief: RHEL upstream. PQC features preview in CentOS Stream before RHEL GA. OpenSSL 3.x + crypto-policies base for system-wide quantum-safe configuration.

pqc_capability_description: ChromeOS (160M+ devices) inherits ML-KEM-768 hybrid TLS 1.3 key exchange from the Chrome browser (enabled by default since v124, 2024). Google's Verified Boot uses SHA-256/RSA for firmware signing — a migration target for ML-DSA. ChromeOS Flex extends PQC TLS to enterprise repurposed hardware. System-level PQC beyond TLS depends on BoringSSL integration.
product_brief: Google OS on 160M+ Chromebooks. ML-KEM hybrid TLS active via Chrome since v124. Secure boot signing migration to ML-DSA is a future roadmap item.

pqc_capability_description: NixOS is a declarative Linux distribution with reproducible builds. nixpkgs includes liboqs and oqs-provider packages enabling ML-KEM/ML-DSA via OpenSSL 3.x provider. The declarative configuration model enables system-wide PQC policy enforcement through Nix modules. Reproducible builds assist in PQC migration auditability.
product_brief: Declarative reproducible Linux. liboqs + oqs-provider in nixpkgs enables ML-KEM/ML-DSA. Reproducible builds aid PQC migration audit trail.

pqc_capability_description: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP6 ships OpenSSL 3.x with FIPS 140-3 certified module. SLES 16 (roadmap 2026) includes native PQC algorithm support via crypto-policies and a quantum-safe system profile. SUSE's quantum-safe initiative targets enterprise workloads requiring ML-KEM for TLS and ML-DSA for package signing.
product_brief: Enterprise Linux with FIPS 140-3 OpenSSL. SLES 16 roadmap includes native PQC system profiles. Critical for SAP HANA and regulated enterprise workloads.

pqc_capability_description: Arch Linux is a rolling-release distribution shipping current OpenSSL (3.4.x as of 2025). The liboqs and oqs-provider packages are available in the AUR (Arch User Repository). Arch's rolling model means PQC library updates propagate quickly. Popular in research and development environments. No system-wide PQC crypto policy framework.
product_brief: Rolling-release Linux with current OpenSSL. liboqs available via AUR. Fast PQC library propagation benefits developers and researchers.

pqc_capability_description: Delinea Secret Server is an enterprise privileged access management (PAM) solution for credential vaulting and session management. Relies on AES-256 for secret encryption and TLS for transport. PQC migration requires TLS upgrade (ML-KEM hybrid) and key encryption layer update. Delinea has not published a specific PQC timeline as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Enterprise PAM credential vault. AES-256 secrets storage + classical TLS. PQC migration requires transport and key encryption layer upgrade.

pqc_capability_description: BeyondTrust Privilege Management provides endpoint PAM, privileged remote access, and password management. Cryptographic operations rely on TLS 1.3 for transport and AES-256 for credential storage. PQC roadmap tied to underlying platform TLS library upgrades. No dedicated PQC timeline published.
product_brief: Enterprise endpoint PAM and remote access. PQC migration depends on TLS library upgrade. No dedicated PQC roadmap published as of Q1 2026.

pqc_capability_description: JumpCloud is a cloud-based directory platform offering LDAP, RADIUS, SSO, MDM, and device management. All communications use TLS 1.3. PQC migration requires ML-KEM hybrid for all RADIUS/LDAP/TLS connections. JumpCloud has engaged in NIST PQC working groups. No GA PQC release announced as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Cloud directory platform replacing on-prem Active Directory. PQC migration needed for all LDAP/RADIUS/TLS communications. No GA PQC release as of Q1 2026.

pqc_capability_description: Teleport provides zero-trust access for SSH, Kubernetes, databases, and web applications using certificate-based mTLS. Built on Go's crypto/tls which tracks upstream Go PQC support. Teleport 16+ targets ML-KEM hybrid TLS when Go's standard library enables it. Certificate issuance (X.509) will need ML-DSA migration.
product_brief: Zero-trust access platform for SSH/K8s/databases. PQC via Go stdlib ML-KEM support. Certificate issuance requires ML-DSA migration.

pqc_capability_description: HashiCorp Boundary is a privileged access management tool for dynamic infrastructure using identity-based access. Uses mTLS for all control plane and data plane communications. PQC roadmap aligned with HashiCorp's broader Vault PQC initiative. ML-KEM hybrid TLS planned when Go standard library supports it by default.
product_brief: Identity-based privileged access for dynamic infrastructure. PQC via HashiCorp portfolio roadmap — ML-KEM hybrid TLS when Go stdlib enables it.

pqc_capability_description: Cloudflare Zero Trust (formerly Cloudflare Access + Gateway) provides secure web gateway, CASB, and remote access. Cloudflare enabled ML-KEM-768 hybrid TLS 1.3 on its global network in 2024. Cloudflare WARP client supports PQC-protected tunnels. Cloudflare is a leader in real-world PQC deployment — 20%+ of TLS connections use ML-KEM hybrid.
primary_platforms: Cloud (SaaS) Windows macOS Linux iOS Android
product_brief: Zero-trust network access with ML-KEM hybrid TLS active on Cloudflare's global network. WARP client provides PQC-protected tunnels. Production PQC at global scale.

pqc_capability_description: Zscaler Zero Trust Exchange (ZTE) provides cloud-native secure internet access, private access (ZTNA), and digital experience monitoring. All traffic flows through Zscaler's proxy. PQC migration requires ML-KEM for TLS interception infrastructure and ML-DSA for certificate chain. Zscaler has engaged in NIST PQC working groups but has not announced GA PQC dates.
product_brief: Cloud-native SASE platform with TLS inspection at scale. PQC migration critical — handles TLS interception for millions of enterprise users. No GA PQC date published.

pqc_capability_description: Splunk Enterprise is the leading SIEM/observability platform. Splunk can ingest and analyze cryptographic inventory data (TLS versions, cipher suites, certificate expiry) to support PQC migration planning. Splunk itself communicates over TLS 1.3 (classical). No PQC algorithm support in Splunk core. The Splunk Security Essentials app can be configured for PQC readiness dashboards.
product_brief: Leading SIEM platform. Valuable for PQC migration planning via cryptographic inventory dashboards. Core Splunk communications are classical TLS — no native PQC.

pqc_capability_description: Elastic Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) is an open-source SIEM and observability platform. Like Splunk, Elastic can serve as a PQC migration observability layer — ingesting TLS handshake logs, certificate data, and cipher suite analytics. Elastic itself uses TLS 1.3 (classical) for node-to-node and client communications. No PQC algorithm support.
product_brief: Open-source SIEM and observability. Useful as PQC migration monitoring layer for TLS/cipher analytics. Core communications are classical TLS — no native PQC.

pqc_capability_description: Suricata is an open-source network threat detection engine supporting IDS, IPS, and NSM. PQC relevance is for TLS fingerprinting and detecting PQC cipher suites in network traffic. Suricata 8.x development includes detection rules for ML-KEM hybrid TLS handshakes and malformed PQC packets. Used by SOCs to monitor the PQC transition in enterprise networks.
product_brief: Open-source IDS/IPS. PQC role: detection rules for ML-KEM hybrid TLS handshakes in network traffic. Used by SOCs to monitor enterprise PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: Zeek (formerly Bro) is a network analysis framework used for TLS/SSL inspection, DNS monitoring, and protocol analysis. Zeek 6.x includes TLS fingerprinting for ML-KEM hybrid cipher suites. SOC teams use Zeek to detect PQC algorithm negotiation in TLS handshakes and monitor migration progress. Does not implement PQC algorithms itself.
product_brief: Network analysis framework for TLS/protocol inspection. PQC role: detect ML-KEM hybrid cipher suites in TLS handshakes. Monitors enterprise PQC migration progress.

pqc_capability_description: Tenable Nessus is the world's most widely deployed vulnerability scanner. PQC relevance: Nessus includes plugins for TLS cipher suite enumeration, weak algorithm detection, and certificate analysis. Tenable One (enterprise) includes cryptographic inventory capabilities for PQC migration readiness assessment. Nessus itself communicates via classical TLS.
product_brief: Leading vulnerability scanner with TLS/cipher suite assessment plugins. Used for PQC migration readiness scans. Tenable One provides enterprise-scale cryptographic inventory.

pqc_capability_description: Rapid7 InsightVM is a vulnerability management and risk scoring platform. InsightVM includes TLS/SSL configuration assessment and certificate management visibility. The InsightVM Quantum Readiness dashboard (announced 2025) maps cryptographic dependencies across the enterprise. InsightIDR (SIEM component) supports PQC algorithm detection in network logs.
product_brief: Vulnerability management with quantum readiness dashboard. Maps cryptographic dependencies for PQC migration planning. TLS/cert assessment across enterprise endpoints.

pqc_capability_description: Qualys Cloud Platform provides continuous security monitoring, vulnerability management, and compliance assessment. Qualys SSL Labs (free) and Qualys VM Enterprise include TLS cipher suite grading, certificate inventory, and weak algorithm detection. Useful for PQC migration baseline assessment. Qualys has not published PQC-specific detection capabilities as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Cloud-native vulnerability management. SSL Labs provides public TLS cipher grading. Enterprise VM provides cryptographic inventory for PQC migration baseline.

pqc_capability_description: Google Workspace Identity includes SSO, SAML, OAuth 2.0, and LDAP services for enterprise users. Google's infrastructure already uses ML-KEM hybrid TLS on all Google services (enabled 2024). Authentication tokens and SAML assertions still use classical RSA/ECC signing. Google has not announced a specific ML-DSA migration timeline for identity tokens.
product_brief: Google enterprise identity. ML-KEM hybrid TLS active on all Google infrastructure. Identity token signing (SAML/OAuth) still classical — ML-DSA migration timeline pending.

pqc_capability_description: AWS IAM is the access control service for all AWS resources. AWS has enabled ML-KEM hybrid TLS 1.3 on the AWS API endpoints and console. IAM policy signing and STS token signing (SigV4) use classical HMAC-SHA256. AWS has not announced ML-DSA migration for SigV4 or IAM credentials. AWS Nitro Enclaves and KMS are separate PQC migration targets.
product_brief: AWS access control service. ML-KEM hybrid TLS active on AWS API endpoints. SigV4 token signing remains classical — PQC migration for credentials pending.

pqc_capability_description: CrowdStrike Falcon is a leading EDR/XDR platform. Falcon sensor communicates with the Falcon Cloud via TLS 1.3. CrowdStrike has announced PQC readiness for sensor-to-cloud communications as part of its cryptographic agility initiative. Falcon Exposure Management includes cryptographic inventory capabilities. No GA PQC sensor release as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Market-leading EDR/XDR. PQC sensor transport roadmap announced. Falcon Exposure Management provides cryptographic inventory for PQC migration planning.

pqc_capability_description: Palo Alto Cortex XDR is an extended detection and response platform integrating endpoint, network, and cloud telemetry. Agent-to-cloud communications use TLS 1.3. PQC roadmap aligned with Palo Alto's quantum-safe initiative (separate from PAN-OS ML-KEM support). Cortex XSIAM includes cryptographic exposure detection features.
product_brief: XDR platform with endpoint/network/cloud telemetry. PQC transport roadmap aligned with Palo Alto quantum-safe initiative. Cryptographic exposure detection in Cortex XSIAM.

pqc_capability_description: Citrix Virtual Apps and Desktops (CVAD) delivers virtualized applications and desktops over ICA/HDX protocol. All client-server communications use TLS 1.3.
