PQC migration requires Citrix Gateway (ADC) ML-KEM TLS support and ICA protocol channel encryption upgrade. Citrix has not published a specific PQC roadmap as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Enterprise VDI and app delivery. PQC migration requires Citrix Gateway ML-KEM and ICA protocol encryption upgrade. No GA PQC roadmap published.

pqc_capability_description: VMware Horizon (now Broadcom) provides VDI and DaaS using the Blast Extreme protocol over HTTPS/WebSocket. TLS 1.3 is used for all management and display protocol connections. PQC migration requires Unified Access Gateway (UAG) ML-KEM support and Blast protocol encryption upgrade. No PQC timeline published under Broadcom ownership.
product_brief: Enterprise VDI/DaaS platform. PQC migration requires UAG gateway ML-KEM and Blast Extreme protocol upgrade. No PQC roadmap under Broadcom ownership.

pqc_capability_description: Apache Guacamole is an open-source clientless remote desktop gateway supporting VNC, RDP, and SSH over HTTPS. Guacamole uses TLS for the browser-to-gateway connection. PQC support depends on the underlying Java TLS stack (JSSE) and SSL termination layer (Nginx/Apache HTTPD). ML-KEM support will propagate from Java 22+ TLS improvements. No dedicated PQC release planned.
product_brief: Open-source clientless remote desktop gateway. PQC via Java JSSE TLS upgrade. Widely deployed in self-hosted remote access scenarios.

pqc_capability_description: AnyDesk provides remote desktop software using its proprietary DeskRT codec with TLS 1.2/1.3 transport. AnyDesk is incorporated in Germany and subject to BSI (Federal Office for Information Security) PQC guidance. No public PQC roadmap announced as of Q1 2026. Transport upgrade to ML-KEM hybrid TLS expected to follow BSI TR-02102 updates.
product_brief: Remote desktop software. German company subject to BSI PQC guidance. Transport PQC expected to follow BSI TR-02102 ML-KEM recommendation adoption.

pqc_capability_description: PuTTY is the most widely used SSH client on Windows. PuTTY 0.82+ (development) includes experimental support for SSH post-quantum key exchange (sntrup761x25519-sha512 hybrid) following OpenSSH's implementation. ML-KEM hybrid key exchange is on the PuTTY development roadmap. Widely deployed in enterprise and government Windows environments.
product_brief: Most widely used Windows SSH client. Experimental PQC KEX (sntrup761x25519) in development. ML-KEM hybrid SSH on roadmap for enterprise/government transition.

pqc_capability_description: Paramiko is the standard Python SSH implementation used by Ansible, Fabric, and countless automation scripts. Paramiko 3.x uses classical RSA/ECDSA/Ed25519 for host keys and Diffie-Hellman/ECDH for key exchange. No PQC support implemented or announced. PQC migration requires waiting for upstream SSH protocol PQC KEX standards and Paramiko implementation.
product_brief: Standard Python SSH library. Used by Ansible and automation tooling. No PQC support — millions of automation scripts depend on classical SSH.

pqc_capability_description: AsyncSSH is a Python async SSH library providing both client and server capabilities. AsyncSSH 2.18+ includes support for the sntrup761x25519-sha512@openssh.com post-quantum hybrid key exchange algorithm, mirroring OpenSSH's implementation. ML-KEM migration planned when IETF SSH PQC KEX draft is finalized.
product_brief: Python async SSH library with sntrup761 hybrid KEX support. More PQC-advanced than synchronous Paramiko. Used in modern async Python automation.

pqc_capability_description: Microsoft RDS provides Windows desktop and application delivery over RDP protocol. RDP 10.x uses TLS 1.3 for transport. PQC migration requires Windows Server RDP stack to support ML-KEM hybrid TLS and RDP protocol encryption upgrade. Microsoft has included RDS in its CNSA 2.0 migration roadmap targeting 2027. Windows Server 2025 Preview includes early PQC TLS work.
product_brief: Windows RDP-based remote desktop. PQC on Microsoft CNSA 2.0 roadmap targeting 2027. Windows Server 2025 includes early ML-KEM TLS groundwork.

pqc_capability_description: Snort is the original open-source intrusion detection system, now maintained by Cisco. Snort 3.x includes TLS inspector capabilities. PQC-relevant detection rules for hybrid ML-KEM cipher suites are planned for Snort 3.2+ community ruleset. Used in academic and enterprise networks for network-level cryptographic monitoring.
product_brief: Original open-source IDS/IPS maintained by Cisco. PQC detection rules for ML-KEM hybrid TLS in development for Snort 3.2+ community ruleset.

pqc_capability_description: Cisco ASA is the most widely deployed enterprise firewall platform. ASA Software uses classical IKEv2/IPsec for VPN and TLS for management. Cisco has published a quantum-safe networking roadmap targeting ASA/Firepower NGFW ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 support for 2027 as part of CNSA 2.0 compliance. Current ASA 9.x does not support PQC key exchange.
product_brief: Most deployed enterprise firewall. Cisco quantum-safe roadmap targets ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 VPN for 2027 (CNSA 2.0). Critical migration dependency for enterprise perimeter security.

pqc_capability_description: Cisco Meraki MX is a cloud-managed next-generation firewall/SD-WAN platform used heavily in branch offices and SMB/enterprise deployments. AutoVPN uses IKEv2/IPsec with classical algorithms. Meraki cloud dashboard communications use TLS 1.3 (classical). Cisco has not published a Meraki-specific PQC timeline — expected to follow Cisco's overall quantum-safe roadmap.
product_brief: Cloud-managed NGFW/SD-WAN for branch offices. AutoVPN uses classical IKEv2. PQC follows Cisco's quantum-safe roadmap — no specific Meraki timeline published.

pqc_capability_description: Juniper SRX Series firewalls run Junos OS and provide enterprise-grade IPsec VPN, IPS, and application security. Juniper has published a quantum-safe roadmap including ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 for Junos OS targeting 2026. Juniper Networks is part of the ETSI QKD and NIST PQC working groups. SRX is widely deployed in carrier-grade and enterprise data center perimeters.
product_brief: Enterprise/carrier NGFW on Junos OS. ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 planned for Junos 2026. Juniper active in ETSI QKD and NIST PQC working groups.

pqc_capability_description: Fortinet FortiGate is the market-leading enterprise firewall/NGFW with the largest global market share. FortiOS 7.6+ roadmap includes quantum-safe VPN using ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 for IPsec tunnels. Fortinet FortiGuard Labs has published PQC threat intelligence. No GA ML-KEM release as of Q1 2026. FortiGate is critical for APAC enterprise and government networks.
product_brief: World's most widely deployed NGFW by units. ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 VPN in FortiOS 7.6+ roadmap. Critical migration dependency for enterprise and APAC government networks.

pqc_capability_description: Arista EOS powers Arista data center and campus switches/routers. EOS uses TLS 1.3 for management (eAPI, gNMI, gRPC) and BGP/OSPF routing protocols. PQC migration targets management plane TLS (ML-KEM) and BGPsec signing (ML-DSA). Arista has engaged in IETF PQC for BGP security working groups. No GA PQC release as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Data center network OS. PQC targets management TLS (ML-KEM) and BGPsec signing (ML-DSA). Arista active in IETF PQC-BGP working groups.

pqc_capability_description: MikroTik RouterOS is a widely deployed network operating system for routers and switches, popular in ISPs and SMBs globally (especially Eastern Europe, Latin America, Asia). RouterOS uses IKEv2/IPsec for VPN with classical algorithms and TLS for management (Winbox, WebFig, API). No PQC roadmap published. MikroTik has not engaged with NIST PQC standardization process.
product_brief: Widely deployed ISP/SMB router OS. No PQC roadmap. Classical IKEv2/IPsec VPN. Millions of deployed units represent a significant PQC migration long tail.

pqc_capability_description: OpenWrt is an open-source Linux-based firmware for routers and embedded network devices. OpenWrt 23.05+ ships OpenSSL 3.x enabling PQC via liboqs-provider package. The WireGuard package (included by default) does not yet support PQC KEX. strongSwan package in OpenWrt provides experimental ML-KEM IPsec. Used in home, SMB, and research networks.
product_brief: Open-source Linux router firmware. OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider available. strongSwan provides experimental ML-KEM IPsec. Widely used in research and SMB networks.

pqc_capability_description: pfSense CE is an open-source firewall/router based on FreeBSD. IPsec VPN uses strongSwan with classical algorithms. PQC support would require FreeBSD strongSwan with ML-KEM enabled — currently not in pfSense base packages. Netgate (commercial maintainer) has not published a PQC roadmap for pfSense CE.
product_brief: Open-source FreeBSD firewall. No PQC support. Widely deployed in SMB and education. PQC requires FreeBSD strongSwan upgrade not yet in pfSense packages.

pqc_capability_description: OPNsense is an open-source FreeBSD-based firewall forked from pfSense. OPNsense 24.7+ includes strongSwan with experimental ML-KEM (Kyber) support for IKEv2 IPsec tunnels via plugin. The OPNsense team has been more proactive than pfSense on PQC integration. ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 available as experimental feature in 24.7+.
product_brief: Open-source FreeBSD firewall. Experimental ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 via strongSwan plugin in 24.7+. More PQC-forward than pfSense.

pqc_capability_description: NGINX Plus is the commercial version of the NGINX web server and load balancer, widely used for TLS termination. NGINX Plus R32+ (2024) supports OpenSSL 3.x which enables ML-KEM hybrid TLS when built with liboqs-provider. F5 (NGINX owner) has published PQC TLS guidance using NGINX Plus. Critical for TLS termination in enterprise application delivery.
product_brief: Commercial NGINX load balancer/TLS terminator. ML-KEM hybrid TLS via OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider in R32+. Critical PQC migration point for enterprise TLS termination.

pqc_capability_description: Traefik is a cloud-native reverse proxy and load balancer widely used as Kubernetes ingress controller. Built in Go, Traefik's TLS support depends on Go's crypto/tls package. Go 1.23+ includes experimental ML-KEM-768 hybrid TLS 1.3. Traefik will inherit PQC support when Go's stdlib enables it by default (expected Go 1.25). Used in Docker Swarm and Kubernetes environments.
product_brief: Cloud-native Kubernetes ingress and reverse proxy. PQC via Go stdlib ML-KEM when enabled by default (~Go 1.25). Standard ingress controller for containerized deployments.

pqc_capability_description: Kubernetes is the standard container orchestration platform powering cloud-native deployments. Kubernetes API server uses TLS 1.3 (classical) for kubectl and component communications. Pod-to-pod encryption requires a service mesh (Istio, Linkerd). Kubernetes 1.31+ roadmap includes post-quantum TLS for the API server via Go stdlib. Full PQC requires: API server TLS, etcd TLS, and service mesh mTLS all upgraded. No PQC-ready release as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Standard container orchestration platform. No PQC yet — API server TLS, etcd TLS, and service mesh mTLS all require upgrade. Full PQC migration is a multi-layer dependency chain.

pqc_capability_description: Istio is the most widely deployed Kubernetes service mesh, providing automatic mTLS for pod-to-pod communication. Istio's data plane is Envoy proxy. As Envoy adopts ML-KEM hybrid TLS, Istio will inherit PQC mTLS support. Istio control plane (istiod) uses TLS for certificate issuance — will require ML-DSA migration. Istio 1.22+ roadmap includes PQC mTLS experimental support.
product_brief: Leading Kubernetes service mesh with automatic mTLS. PQC via Envoy ML-KEM inheritance. Control plane certificate issuance needs ML-DSA migration. Critical for zero-trust container networking.

pqc_capability_description: Linkerd is a lightweight CNCF service mesh focused on simplicity and performance. Built in Rust, Linkerd uses the linkerd2-proxy (Rust) with rustls for TLS. Rustls has added ML-KEM support in 0.23+. Linkerd's Rust-based proxy can inherit ML-KEM hybrid TLS when rustls enables it by default. Simpler PQC migration path than Istio due to Rust cryptography stack.
product_brief: Lightweight CNCF service mesh in Rust. PQC via rustls ML-KEM inheritance. Simpler migration path than Istio/Envoy — Rust stack tracks PQC faster than C++ BoringSSL.

pqc_capability_description: HashiCorp Consul Connect provides service mesh capabilities with automatic mTLS using Envoy as the data plane proxy. PQC migration path mirrors Envoy's ML-KEM adoption. Consul certificate authority (built-in or Vault-backed) will require ML-DSA migration. Part of HashiCorp's broader quantum-safe portfolio roadmap.
product_brief: Service mesh with Envoy data plane. PQC via Envoy ML-KEM inheritance. Consul CA needs ML-DSA migration. Part of HashiCorp quantum-safe portfolio roadmap.

pqc_capability_description: OpenVPN is the most widely deployed open-source VPN solution. OpenVPN 2.7+ development includes experimental post-quantum key exchange using ML-KEM-768 hybrid mode. The OpenVPN 3.x series (library-based) targets PQC via OpenSSL 3.x provider integration. OpenVPN Cloud (commercial) is expected to enable PQC in 2026. Critical migration dependency for millions of enterprise and consumer VPN deployments.
product_brief: Most widely deployed open-source VPN. Experimental ML-KEM hybrid in 2.7+ development. OpenVPN Cloud targets PQC in 2026. Critical for enterprise and consumer VPN migration.

pqc_capability_description: WireGuard is a modern VPN protocol using Noise protocol framework with X25519 for key exchange. The Post-Quantum WireGuard (PQWireGuard) extension adds ML-KEM-768 to the handshake. Mullvad VPN (already in catalog) ships PQ WireGuard. WireGuard kernel module does not natively support PQC — it requires the userspace PQ extension. IETF draft for PQ WireGuard standardization is in progress.
product_brief: Modern VPN protocol. PQ WireGuard extension adds ML-KEM-768 hybrid handshake. Kernel module is classical-only — PQC requires userspace PQ extension.

pqc_capability_description: Cisco Secure Client (formerly AnyConnect) is the most widely deployed enterprise VPN client. Uses SSL/TLS and IKEv2/IPsec for VPN tunnels. Cisco's quantum-safe roadmap includes ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 for Secure Client targeting 2027 as part of CNSA 2.0 compliance. Deployed in 100M+ enterprise endpoints. Migration requires both client and headend (ASA/FTD) upgrade simultaneously.
product_brief: Most deployed enterprise VPN client (100M+ endpoints). ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 planned for 2027 (CNSA 2.0). Requires simultaneous client + ASA/FTD headend upgrade.

pqc_capability_description: Palo Alto GlobalProtect is the enterprise VPN client for Palo Alto firewall infrastructure. GlobalProtect uses IPsec/TLS with classical algorithms. PAN-OS 12.1 Orion (already in catalog) includes ML-KEM for cipher proxy but GlobalProtect VPN tunnels are a separate migration target. Palo Alto has committed to GlobalProtect ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 in a future PAN-OS release.
product_brief: Palo Alto enterprise VPN client. ML-KEM hybrid IKEv2 planned in future PAN-OS (separate from existing cipher proxy support). Requires PAN-OS + client simultaneous upgrade.

pqc_capability_description: Samsung Networks provides 5G RAN and core network equipment competing with Ericsson and Nokia. Samsung has announced PQC integration aligned with 3GPP Release 20/21 specifications for SA2/SA3 security. Deployed in US (Verizon, T-Mobile), Japan, and South Korea networks. No GA PQC release as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: Samsung 5G RAN/core deployed at Verizon, T-Mobile, and APAC carriers. PQC per 3GPP Rel-20/21 roadmap. No GA PQC release as of Q1 2026.

pqc_capability_description: Mavenir provides cloud-native 5G RAN (vRAN/Open RAN) and core network functions. As a cloud-native solution, Mavenir's Kubernetes-based deployment inherits PQC from the service mesh and TLS layer. Mavenir has committed to 3GPP quantum-safe standards alignment. Deployed in US DoD, UK, and emerging markets.
product_brief: Cloud-native Open RAN platform. PQC via Kubernetes/service mesh inheritance + 3GPP Rel-20/21 roadmap. Deployed in US DoD and emerging market carriers.

pqc_capability_description: NEC Corporation provides 5G core network solutions and Open RAN equipment, particularly in Japan and APAC markets. NEC has collaborated with NTT on quantum-safe 5G research and aligned with 3GPP SA3 quantum security specifications. PQC integration planned per 3GPP Release 20/21 timeline.
product_brief: NEC 5G core and Open RAN for APAC markets. PQC research with NTT. 3GPP SA3 quantum security alignment for Rel-20/21.

pqc_capability_description: Rakuten Symphony provides cloud-native Open RAN and 5G core solutions based on Rakuten's own network deployment. The Symworld platform runs on Kubernetes and uses standard cloud-native security. PQC migration inherits from Kubernetes/Istio mTLS upgrades plus 3GPP SA3 specifications. Rakuten is a founding member of O-RAN Alliance.
product_brief: Cloud-native Open RAN from Rakuten. PQC via Kubernetes/Istio inheritance + 3GPP Rel-20/21. O-RAN Alliance founding member.

pqc_capability_description: Apache Traffic Server (ATS) is a high-performance HTTP caching proxy and load balancer used by major CDNs (Comcast, LinkedIn, Yahoo). ATS 10.x uses OpenSSL 3.x enabling ML-KEM hybrid TLS 1.3 via liboqs-provider. The ATS community has published PQC TLS integration guides. Critical for CDN operators managing TLS termination at scale.
product_brief: High-performance CDN proxy/load balancer. ML-KEM hybrid TLS via OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider in ATS 10.x. Used by Comcast, LinkedIn for TLS at CDN scale.

pqc_support: Yes (All NIST PQC standards + round 4 candidates; ML-KEM; ML-DSA; SLH-DSA; FN-DSA; HQC; Classic McEliece; BIKE)
pqc_capability_description: The OQS OpenSSL provider (liboqs-provider) is the canonical bridge from liboqs to OpenSSL 3.x. It provides all NIST-standardized PQC algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, FN-DSA) plus round 4 candidates (HQC, BIKE, Classic McEliece) as an OpenSSL provider plugin. Enables PQC TLS 1.3, PQC X.509 certificates, and PQC key operations in any application using OpenSSL 3.x without code changes. Maintained by the Open Quantum Safe project.
product_brief: Canonical liboqs-to-OpenSSL bridge. Enables all NIST PQC algorithms in any OpenSSL 3.x application. Zero code changes required for apps using OpenSSL API.

pqc_capability_description: PyCryptodome is the primary Python cryptographic library for self-contained crypto operations (not TLS). Provides AES, RSA, ECC, SHA families, and DH. No PQC support as of Q1 2026. PyCryptodome development team has not published a PQC roadmap. Python applications requiring PQC should use the pqcrypto package (already in catalog) or call OpenSSL 3.x via PyOpenSSL.
product_brief: Primary Python standalone crypto library. No PQC. Python apps should migrate to pqcrypto package or PyOpenSSL + OpenSSL 3.x for PQC operations.

pqc_capability_description: Crypto++ is a comprehensive C++ cryptographic library used in academic and industrial applications. Crypto++ 8.9 includes ECDSA, RSA, AES, SHA-3 families but no NIST PQC algorithms (ML-KEM/ML-DSA). The maintainer has stated PQC support is planned but no timeline published. C++ applications using Crypto++ for key exchange or signatures require migration to a PQC-capable library.
product_brief: Comprehensive C++ crypto library. No PQC support. C++ applications using Crypto++ for KEX/signatures need migration to OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider.

pqc_capability_description: libssh is a C SSH library used by KDE, QEMU, Cyberduck, and others. libssh 0.11+ development branch includes experimental sntrup761x25519 hybrid key exchange following OpenSSH. ML-KEM hybrid SSH KEX planned when IETF SSH PQC draft finalizes. Used as a dependency in many enterprise tools that require programmatic SSH access.
product_brief: C SSH library used by KDE, QEMU, Cyberduck. Experimental PQC KEX in 0.11+. Migration path for tools using libssh for SSH session management.

pqc_capability_description: Apache Tomcat is the most widely deployed Java servlet container. Tomcat 11.x uses Java's JSSE (Java Secure Socket Extension) for TLS. PQC support depends on the Java Runtime TLS implementation — Java 22+ includes experimental ML-KEM support. Tomcat 11 with Java 22+ enables ML-KEM hybrid TLS without configuration changes. Full PQC including certificate chains requires Java ML-DSA support (Java 24+ roadmap).
product_brief: Most deployed Java servlet container. PQC via Java JSSE ML-KEM in Java 22+ (no code changes). ML-DSA certificate chain requires Java 24+ roadmap item.

pqc_capability_description: Eclipse Jetty is an embeddable Java web server and servlet container used by Jenkins, Hadoop, Eclipse IDE, and many enterprise frameworks. Jetty 12.x uses Java JSSE for TLS. PQC inherits from Java runtime (ML-KEM in Java 22+). Jetty's embedded model means PQC migration can be driven by upgrading the JRE without application code changes.
product_brief: Embeddable Java web server used in Jenkins, Hadoop, Eclipse. PQC via Java JSSE ML-KEM (Java 22+) with no code changes. Key dependency in enterprise Java toolchains.

pqc_capability_description: WildFly (formerly JBoss AS) is a Red Hat-sponsored open-source Java EE application server. WildFly 34+ uses Undertow for HTTP and Java JSSE for TLS. PQC inherits from Java runtime (ML-KEM in Java 22+). WildFly's integration with Red Hat Keycloak and Red Hat SSO means IdP PQC migration is coupled with WildFly upgrades. Used in Red Hat enterprise deployments.
product_brief: Red Hat Java EE application server. PQC via Java JSSE ML-KEM (Java 22+). Coupled with Keycloak/Red Hat SSO for IdP migration.

pqc_capability_description: Payara Server is a commercial fork of GlassFish providing Jakarta EE support. Payara 6.x uses Java JSSE. PQC inherits from Java runtime (ML-KEM in Java 22+). Payara is used in financial services and UK government deployments. Payara has not published an independent PQC roadmap beyond Java platform dependency.
product_brief: Commercial GlassFish fork for Jakarta EE. PQC via Java JSSE (Java 22+). Used in UK government and financial services.

pqc_capability_description: GitHub Actions is the most widely used CI/CD platform. GitHub.com has enabled ML-KEM hybrid TLS (via Cloudflare) on API endpoints. GitHub Actions runners communicate via HTTPS (classical TLS). No dedicated PQC GitHub Actions exist for code signing or artifact verification. Sigstore (cosign, already in catalog) provides PQC-ready artifact signing independent of the CI platform.
product_brief: Most widely used CI/CD platform. Platform HTTPS via Cloudflare ML-KEM hybrid TLS. No native PQC signing actions — use sigstore/cosign for artifact signing.

pqc_capability_description: GitLab CI/CD provides integrated pipelines for GitLab repositories. GitLab.com uses TLS 1.3 (classical) for API communications. Self-hosted GitLab can be configured with OpenSSL 3.x for PQC TLS. GitLab has not published native PQC pipeline features. Artifact signing uses GPG/PGP (classical) or sigstore integration.
product_brief: Integrated CI/CD in GitLab. Self-hosted instances configurable with OpenSSL 3.x PQC TLS. No native PQC signing — integrate sigstore for artifact verification.

pqc_capability_description: Jenkins is the most widely deployed self-hosted CI/CD automation server. Jenkins uses Java JSSE for TLS. PQC TLS inherits from Java runtime (ML-KEM in Java 22+). The Jenkins community has not published native PQC features. Artifact signing via GPG plugins is classical — sigstore/cosign integration plugins are emerging.
product_brief: Most deployed self-hosted CI/CD server. PQC TLS via Java JSSE (Java 22+). Artifact signing migration needs sigstore/cosign plugin.

pqc_capability_description: Azure DevOps (Pipelines, Repos, Artifacts) is Microsoft's enterprise CI/CD and source control platform. Azure DevOps cloud communications use TLS 1.3. PQC migration aligned with Microsoft's CNSA 2.0 roadmap targeting 2027. Azure Artifacts signing uses classical code signing certificates. Microsoft Trusted Signing (formerly Azure Code Signing) will incorporate ML-DSA when available.
product_brief: Microsoft enterprise CI/CD. PQC on CNSA 2.0 roadmap targeting 2027. Azure Trusted Signing will adopt ML-DSA for artifact signing.

pqc_capability_description: CircleCI is a cloud CI/CD platform. CircleCI API communications use TLS 1.3 (classical). No native PQC CI features. Organizations using CircleCI for build pipelines must implement PQC artifact signing independently (sigstore/cosign in pipeline steps). CircleCI has not published a PQC roadmap.
product_brief: Cloud CI/CD platform. Classical TLS only. No native PQC — implement sigstore/cosign in pipeline steps for artifact signing migration.

pqc_capability_description: OAuth 2.0 and OIDC are the standard protocols for API authorization and identity federation. JWTs (JSON Web Tokens) use classical RS256, ES256, or PS256 for signatures. RFC 9449 (DPoP) and draft RFC for PQC JWT signatures propose ML-DSA-based JWTs. The IETF OAuth working group is developing PQC JWT signing standards. Migration requires updating all JWT issuers and validators to ML-DSA signatures.
product_brief: Standard API authorization and identity protocol. Classical JWT signatures (RS256/ES256). IETF draft for ML-DSA JWT signatures in development. Critical migration dependency across all API-based systems.

pqc_capability_description: SAML 2.0 (Security Assertion Markup Language) is the enterprise SSO protocol used by most identity providers and service providers. SAML assertions are signed with RSA-2048 or ECDSA. No PQC SAML specification exists as of Q1 2026. Migration to ML-DSA-signed SAML assertions requires updates to all SAML identity providers (Okta, Azure AD, PingFederate) and service providers simultaneously. OASIS TC (SAML standards body) has not initiated a PQC SAML working group.
product_brief: Enterprise SSO protocol with classical RSA/ECDSA assertion signing. No PQC spec. Migration requires simultaneous IdP and SP upgrade — OASIS TC not yet initiated.

pqc_capability_description: PostgreSQL is the world's most advanced open-source relational database. PostgreSQL uses OpenSSL for TLS client/server connections and SSL certificates. PostgreSQL 17+ with OpenSSL 3.4 + liboqs-provider enables ML-KEM hybrid TLS for connections. Data-at-rest encryption relies on filesystem/volume encryption (LUKS, BitLocker) or pgcrypto (already in catalog) which uses classical algorithms. No native PQC storage encryption — relies on external key management and OpenSSL TLS layer.
product_brief: World's most advanced open-source RDBMS. PQC TLS connection encryption via OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider. Data-at-rest PQC requires external KMS and volume encryption upgrade.

pqc_capability_description: MySQL Community Server is the world's most widely deployed open-source database (200M+ installations). MySQL 8.4 uses OpenSSL for TLS connections (default) and supports TDE (Transparent Data Encryption) for InnoDB via keyring plugins. MySQL with OpenSSL 3.x enables ML-KEM hybrid TLS for connections. MySQL Enterprise Encryption (keyring) uses RSA — migration to PQC key wrapping requires Oracle MySQL Enterprise upgrade or custom keyring plugin.
product_brief: 200M+ installations. PQC TLS via OpenSSL 3.x. InnoDB TDE key wrapping remains classical — migration requires Enterprise keyring plugin or KMS integration upgrade.

pqc_capability_description: Redis is an in-memory data store used as a cache, message broker, and database. Redis 7.x supports TLS via OpenSSL for client-server and cluster communications. With OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider, Redis can be built with ML-KEM hybrid TLS. Redis has no native at-rest encryption — data is stored in plaintext in memory (full-disk encryption handles at-rest). Redis Sentinel and Cluster node-to-node TLS also upgradable via OpenSSL.
product_brief: In-memory data store/cache. PQC TLS for client and cluster communications via OpenSSL 3.x + liboqs-provider. No at-rest encryption — depends on OS volume encryption.

pqc_capability_description: MariaDB Server is the open-source fork of MySQL maintained by the MariaDB Foundation. MariaDB 11.x uses OpenSSL for TLS connections and supports Data-at-Rest Encryption (DARE) via the file_key_management and HashiCorp Vault plugins. ML-KEM hybrid TLS is available with OpenSSL 3.x. Key wrapping for DARE uses AES-256 (quantum-safe for symmetric encryption) but the key management layer (Vault, KMS) requires PQC migration.
product_brief: MySQL-compatible open-source RDBMS. PQC TLS via OpenSSL 3.x. DARE with AES-256 (quantum-safe). Key management layer (Vault/KMS) requires separate PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: MPC-CMP based custody platform. Uses threshold signatures (MPC-CMP protocol) for key management. No PQC algorithms or quantum-safe roadmap announced. Open-sourced MPC-CMP protocol in 2023.
product_brief: MPC-CMP based institutional digital asset custody platform supporting hot and cold wallets with threshold signatures.

pqc_capability_description: API-first key orchestration platform supporting MPC TSS and HSM backends. Quantum-safe cryptography support announced via IBM Crypto Express HSM integration. No specific PQC algorithms or timeline disclosed.
product_brief: API-first key orchestration platform with MPC HSM and hybrid custody models. Quantum-safe support planned via IBM HSM integration.

pqc_capability_description: Swiss digital asset infrastructure provider. CSO Jean-Philippe Aumasson is co-designer of SPHINCS+ (SLH-DSA NIST standard). PQC development stated as strategic objective. Regulated by FINMA. Products: Taurus-PROTECT (custody) Taurus-CAPITAL (tokenization) Taurus-PRIME (trading).
product_brief: Swiss FINMA-regulated digital asset custody tokenization and trading platform. CSO co-designed SPHINCS+ PQC standard.

pqc_capability_description: Fintech payment and wallet infrastructure platform (crypto-to-fiat conversion). Not a dedicated crypto custodian. No PQC algorithms or quantum-safe roadmap announced.
product_brief: Cloud-native fintech platform for banking payments and digital wallet infrastructure with crypto capabilities.

pqc_capability_description: TSS 2-of-3 threshold signature custody platform with HSM backing. OCC-chartered national trust bank. Licensed under MiCAR (EU). No PQC roadmap or quantum-safe migration plans announced.
product_brief: OCC-chartered institutional custody platform using TSS 2-of-3 threshold signatures and HSMs for digital asset security.

pqc_capability_description: OCC-chartered crypto bank using MPC+HSM hybrid architecture. Supports 25+ blockchain networks. FIPS 140-2 compliant HSMs. SOC 1 and SOC 2 Type II certified. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: OCC-chartered digital asset bank with MPC+HSM hybrid custody supporting 25+ blockchains and DeFi integration.

pqc_capability_description: NYDFS qualified custodian and MiCA-licensed institutional custody platform. SOC 1 and SOC 2 audited by Deloitte. Standard ECDSA/EdDSA signatures. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: NYDFS qualified custodian and MiCA-licensed institutional crypto custody with SOC 1/2 audits by Deloitte.

pqc_capability_description: MPC-based custody with 3-of-3 threshold signatures. Supports 60+ networks and 600+ digital assets. Cold warm and hot vault configurations. Lloyd's insurance via AON. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: MPC-based institutional custody supporting 600+ digital assets across 60+ networks with Lloyd's insurance coverage.

pqc_capability_description: Hardware-enforced multi-signature custody using HSMs and Ledger devices. Advocates hybrid hardware+MPC approach over standalone MPC. Supports Bitcoin Ethereum Solana. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: Hardware-enforced institutional custody using HSMs and Ledger devices with hybrid hardware+MPC architecture.

pqc_capability_description: Institutional digital asset custody platform (Harmonize) acquired by Ripple in 2023 for $250M. Cross-asset custody trading and DeFi services. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: Institutional digital asset custody platform (Harmonize) acquired by Ripple. Cross-asset custody trading and DeFi.

pqc_capability_description: Singapore MAS-licensed institutional custody supporting 24+ blockchain networks including Bitcoin Ethereum Solana Algorand Cosmos Polkadot Avalanche. Regulated in Dubai Hong Kong Singapore Italy France. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: MAS-licensed institutional digital asset custody and staking supporting 24+ blockchain networks across 5 jurisdictions.

pqc_capability_description: Standard Chartered-backed air-gapped cold storage custody with HSM-embedded entitlement workflows. SOC 1/2 and ISO 27001 certified. Regulated in Hong Kong. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: Standard Chartered-backed institutional custody with air-gapped cold storage and HSM-embedded workflows.

pqc_capability_description: Nomura/CoinShares/Ledger-backed institutional custody using hybrid MPC+HSM architecture. Regulated in Jersey UK Dubai Italy Singapore. No PQC roadmap announced.
product_brief: Nomura/CoinShares/Ledger-backed institutional custody with hybrid MPC+HSM and multi-jurisdiction regulation.

pqc_capability_description: BIP-360 proposes Pay-to-Merkle-Root (P2MR) as first step toward quantum resistance. Removes keypath spend to protect against long-exposure attacks. Post-quantum signature schemes planned for future proposals. Draft stage - no consensus timeline.
product_brief: Reference implementation of the Bitcoin protocol. BIP-360 proposes P2MR address type as first quantum resistance step.

pqc_capability_description: Ethereum Foundation established dedicated PQ team in 2026 with $2M research prize fund. 4-year Strawmap through 2029: replace BLS consensus sigs with hash-based STARK-friendly sigs then upgrade KZG data availability then enable EIP-8141 signature type switching for wallets. Vitalik Buterin published PQC defense roadmap Feb 2026.
product_brief: Primary Ethereum execution client. $2M PQC research fund and 4-year Strawmap for quantum-safe consensus and wallets.

pqc_capability_description: Uses Ed25519 and BLS aggregation which are Shor-vulnerable. Project Eleven ML-DSA testnet demonstrated quantum-resistant transactions. Hash-based Winternitz one-time sigs exist for vault constructions. Full migration deferred until efficient PQ aggregation schemes available. Transaction size increasing to 4096 bytes in 2026.
product_brief: High-throughput blockchain using Ed25519. Project Eleven ML-DSA testnet active. PQ migration research ongoing.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise blockchain framework using ECDSA with P-256. Identity managed via X.509 certificates and MSP (Membership Service Provider). No official PQC roadmap. Academic PQFabric research exists but not integrated. Could inherit PQC from upstream OpenSSL 3.x.
product_brief: Enterprise permissioned blockchain framework using ECDSA P-256. No PQC roadmap but could inherit from OpenSSL.

pqc_capability_description: Ethereum-compatible enterprise blockchain client using secp256k1 (public) and secp256r1 (private networks). Java-based. No PQC roadmap or integration. Could benefit from upstream Ethereum PQC work.
product_brief: Ethereum-compatible enterprise blockchain client supporting secp256k1 and secp256r1 for private networks.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise DLT with SPHINCS hash-based signature scheme available as production option since 2017. Supports EdDSA (default) ECDSA and RSA. Published BPQS (Blockchain Post-Quantum Signature) protocol at IEEE Blockchain 2018. Cryptographic agility built into platform design.
product_brief: Enterprise DLT with SPHINCS quantum-resistant signatures available since 2017. BPQS protocol published at IEEE.

pqc_capability_description: Quantum Resistant Ledger - native quantum-safe blockchain using XMSS (eXtended Merkle Signature Scheme) hash-based signatures from inception (2018). NIST-approved algorithm. V2.0 major update with Audit-Ready Testnet targeting Q1 2026.
product_brief: Native quantum-resistant blockchain using XMSS hash-based signatures. V2.0 testnet targeting Q1 2026.

pqc_capability_description: First blockchain to execute PQ transaction on live mainnet using Falcon (NIST-selected lattice signature) in Q2 2025. Falcon verification added to AVM opcodes. Consensus module verifying Falcon signatures natively planned for 2026. VRF remains ECC-vulnerable.
product_brief: First blockchain with PQ mainnet transaction (Falcon). Native AVM Falcon verification. Consensus PQC planned 2026.

pqc_capability_description: Strategic long-term PQC commitment collaborating with Google Linux and Microsoft Research experts. Leaning toward lattice-based cryptography. Nightstream standard for privacy layer. Proof chain architecture using Mithril + PQ signatures for historical transaction immutability.
product_brief: Research-driven blockchain with strategic PQC commitment. Lattice-based crypto and Nightstream privacy layer planned.

pqc_capability_description: Originally used quantum-safe Winternitz hash-based signatures (2016-2021). Switched to Ed25519 for UX. IOTA 2.0 returns to hash-based signatures by default with Coordicide completion. Incentivized testnet planned 2025.
product_brief: DAG-based DLT returning to quantum-safe hash-based signatures with IOTA 2.0. Pioneer of hash-based sigs at scale.

pqc_capability_description: Multi-chain framework using sr25519 (Ed25519 variant) with modular crypto backend. Substrate design permits swappable cryptographic primitives enabling per-parachain PQC adoption without core protocol changes.
product_brief: Multi-chain framework with modular crypto backends. Parachains can adopt PQC independently via Substrate.

pqc_capability_description: Appchain ecosystem using secp256k1 (accounts) and Ed25519 (validators). DoraFactory pqc-cosmos project adds Dilithium to CometBFT crypto package. Community-driven CosmosSDK replacement with PQC algorithms. Not in core distribution.
product_brief: Appchain ecosystem with community-driven PQC integration via DoraFactory pqc-cosmos (Dilithium in CometBFT).

pqc_capability_description: Move-based blockchain using Ed25519 ECDSA and BLS12-381. AIP-137 proposal integrates SLH-DSA (FIPS 205 hash-based stateless). Backward compatible - Ed25519 accounts unaffected. No new security assumptions required. Proposal stage.
product_brief: Move-based blockchain with AIP-137 proposal for SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) quantum-resistant signatures.

pqc_capability_description: Uses ECDSA (secp256k1 P-256). AIP-QR-001 forum proposal for staged transition to lattice-based cryptography. Research phase with no concrete timeline. Per-subnet PQC adoption paths possible.
product_brief: Subnet-based blockchain with AIP-QR-001 proposal for staged PQC transition. Early exploration phase.

pqc_capability_description: Uses Ed25519 and secp256k1. Architecturally designed for cryptographic agility with seed-based key structure. Inherently more prepared for PQ transitions than account-based chains. No formal PQC roadmap published.
product_brief: Move-based blockchain with crypto-agile architecture. Seed-based key structure enables faster PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: PQC support planned for future releases via OpenSSL 3.5 integration. Currently supports TLS 1.3 with classical algorithms. Hybrid ML-KEM expected in upcoming NSX ALB releases.
product_brief: Application delivery controller and load balancer (formerly Avi Networks / VMware NSX ALB) with L4-L7 load balancing, WAF, and service mesh integration.

pqc_capability_description: Claroty Extended Internet of Things (XIoT) security platform with PQC transition roadmap. Provides OT/ICS asset discovery, vulnerability management, and cryptographic posture visibility for critical infrastructure. PQC-aware network traffic analysis planned for IEC 62351-9 compliance.
target_industries: Energy Utilities Manufacturing Government Critical Infrastructure
product_brief: Leading OT/ICS security platform covering asset discovery, vulnerability management, and network monitoring for critical infrastructure environments navigating PQC transition.

pqc_capability_description: Industrial cybersecurity platform designed for OT environments. Provides ICS/SCADA threat detection, incident response, and cryptographic visibility. Dragos published a PQC readiness assessment guide for ICS operators in 2025, with platform PQC support targeted for 2027.
product_brief: Purpose-built ICS/OT cybersecurity platform providing threat intelligence, detection, and response capabilities for industrial environments including energy and utilities sectors.

pqc_capability_description: OT/IoT network monitoring platform with passive traffic analysis and cryptographic inventory capabilities. Guardian identifies deprecated cryptographic algorithms in ICS protocols (Modbus, DNP3, IEC 61850) and generates PQC migration reports for IEC 62351-9 readiness assessment.
product_brief: OT/IoT security platform offering real-time monitoring, asset inventory, and cryptographic discovery for industrial control system environments.

pqc_capability_description: Ruggedized next-generation firewall designed for harsh industrial and utility environments. FortiOS 7.6 adds ML-KEM hybrid TLS and ML-DSA for OT VPN tunnels (IKEv2/IPsec). Supports IEC 62443 security zones and conduit model for SCADA network segmentation.
product_brief: Industrial-grade NGFW with OT protocol inspection, IEC 62443 compliance, and PQC-ready VPN for energy and utility SCADA network security.

pqc_capability_description: Secure remote access management platform for industrial networks. Provides encrypted tunnels for remote maintenance of SCADA, DCS, and PLC systems. Siemens has indicated PQC-hybrid TLS support for SINEMA RC in 2026 to meet IEC 62443-3-3 SR 1.13 (use of cryptographically strong algorithms) with ML-KEM hybrid mode.
product_brief: Industrial remote access platform enabling secure encrypted connectivity to OT devices and SCADA systems, with PQC-hybrid TLS roadmap for IEC 62443 compliance.

pqc_capability_description: ML supply chain security platform for scanning AI model files (ONNX, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Pickle) for malicious payloads, backdoors, and cryptographic vulnerabilities. PQC roadmap includes ML-DSA-based model signing and ML-KEM-encrypted model artifact storage to secure AI supply chains against quantum-capable adversaries.
product_brief: AI/ML supply chain security platform providing model scanning, provenance verification, and cryptographic integrity checks for machine learning pipelines.

pqc_capability_description: Machine learning model security platform providing adversarial robustness testing, model integrity verification, and runtime threat detection. HiddenLayer (now part of Microsoft) is developing ML-DSA-based model signing capabilities to ensure AI model authenticity and integrity against quantum-capable supply chain attackers.
product_brief: AI security platform for detecting adversarial ML attacks, model tampering, and supply chain compromises with emerging PQC signing capabilities.

pqc_capability_description: AI security posture management (AI-SPM) platform providing risk assessment, data lineage tracking, and cryptographic posture visibility for enterprise AI deployments. Cranium's PQC readiness module assesses encryption protecting training data, model weights, and inference pipelines against harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks.
product_brief: AI security posture management platform assessing cryptographic risks in ML pipelines, model stores, and AI data flows including PQC migration readiness.

pqc_capability_description: LLM security platform providing prompt injection defense, data leakage prevention, and content policy enforcement for AI applications. Lakera Guard protects AI inference endpoints over TLS and benefits from PQC-protected transport to prevent interception of sensitive prompts and model outputs. Lakera is evaluating PQC-aware API gateway integration.
product_brief: LLM security guardrail platform protecting AI applications from prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data leakage over cryptographically protected channels.

pqc_capability_description: AI model firewall providing runtime monitoring, adversarial input detection, and model integrity enforcement. Robust Intelligence (acquired by Cisco) is developing quantum-safe model signing using ML-DSA for continuous model integrity verification and PQC-protected telemetry pipelines to defend AI infrastructure against quantum-capable adversaries.
product_brief: AI security platform providing model firewall, adversarial defense, and cryptographic integrity verification for enterprise machine learning deployments.

software_name: Thales CipherTrust Data Discovery and Classification
pqc_capability_description: Enterprise data discovery and classification platform with cryptographic asset inventory capabilities. Discovers sensitive data and cryptographic material across structured and unstructured data stores. Generates Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) for PQC migration planning. Identifies quantum-vulnerable cryptographic algorithms in data at rest and data flows. Part of the Thales CipherTrust Data Security Platform. Integrates with CipherTrust Manager for unified key and crypto policy management.
product_brief: Enterprise data discovery and classification with cryptographic inventory for PQC migration. Discovers and classifies sensitive data and crypto material across hybrid environments. Generates CBOM for quantum-vulnerability assessment. Part of CipherTrust Data Security Platform.

pqc_capability_description: Software for building a Quantum Delivery Network (QDN). Provides intelligent quantum-safe key routing across extended fiber distances beyond QKD's natural ~300 km limit using trusted repeater nodes. Vendor-neutral — works with QKD hardware from ID Quantique, Toshiba, and other vendors, preventing lock-in. Includes BasejumpSIM cloud-based simulation tool for modeling QDN deployments before physical rollout. Deployed in production with Nokia and ID Quantique for Proximus (Belgian telecom) live hybrid quantum encryption key trial over an optical network.
product_brief: QKD orchestration software enabling quantum-safe key delivery networks beyond 300 km fiber limits. Vendor-neutral, works with ID Quantique and Toshiba QKD hardware. Includes network simulation tool.

pqc_capability_description: Symmetric Key Infrastructure (SKI) combining symmetric pre-shared keys (PSK), ECDH, and post-quantum cryptography (ML-KEM) in a multimodal key establishment approach. Delivers hybrid quantum-safe end-to-end encryption keys without requiring QKD hardware or significant infrastructure changes. Designed as a drop-in complement to BasejumpQDN for organizations in early PQC migration stages or those not yet deploying QKD hardware. Implements evolutionQ MultimodalKES protocol for defense-in-depth protection against quantum and classical threats.
product_brief: Hybrid PQC+PSK symmetric key infrastructure requiring no QKD hardware. Multimodal key establishment combining ML-KEM, ECDH, and pre-shared keys for quantum-safe communications.

pqc_capability_description: Production-grade hybrid post-quantum VPN using IKEv2 with RFC 9370 extensions (the IETF-ratified standard for multiple key exchanges in IKEv2, co-authored by Post-Quantum Ltd). Supports ML-KEM-1024 + Classic McEliece hybrid KEM for defense-in-depth key establishment. Fully backward compatible with existing IPsec/IKEv2 infrastructure — no rip-and-replace required. Crypto-agile architecture allows algorithm updates without redeployment. Deployed protecting NATO communications and active conflict zone drone operations. FIPS 140-3 validation in progress.
product_brief: RFC 9370 hybrid PQC VPN (IKEv2 + ML-KEM + Classic McEliece). NATO-deployed, backward compatible with existing IPsec infrastructure. Co-authored the IETF standard for hybrid PQC key exchange.

pqc_capability_description: REST API and SDK implementing the BLAST (Bounded Leakage-Abuse Symmetric-key Transport) algorithm for generating identical symmetric encryption keys independently at multiple endpoints — without transmitting keys over the network. Eliminates the key distribution problem. Powered by quantum entropy sourced from ICFO (Spain), Los Alamos National Lab, Oak Ridge National Lab, and EPFL (Switzerland). Peer-reviewed algorithm (Prof. Yevgeniy Dodis, NYU). Available as cloud API and on-premises. AWS Marketplace listed. Partnership with Fortanix for multi-source quantum entropy compliance (March 2026). Supports GDPR and CNSA 2.0 compliance needs.
product_brief: Quantum entropy SDK that generates symmetric keys independently at each endpoint — no key transmission required. Multi-source quantum entropy (LANL, ORNL, ICFO, EPFL). AWS Marketplace available.

pqc_capability_description: Autonomous cryptographic inventory and risk assessment tool from ISARA Corporation. Continuously catalogs and monitors cryptographic assets across environments — algorithms, protocols, devices, certificates, and keys. Provides risk-prioritized migration roadmap for transitioning to post-quantum cryptography. Designed as a companion discovery tool to ISARA Radiate (PQC algorithm library), enabling organizations to assess their cryptographic exposure before beginning PQC remediation. Supports enterprise-wide CBOM generation and compliance gap analysis.
product_brief: Cryptographic discovery and risk assessment platform from ISARA. Catalogs all cryptographic assets across environments and provides a risk-prioritized PQC migration roadmap. Companion to ISARA Radiate.

pqc_capability_description: World's largest free automated CA (160M+ active certificates). Uses ACME protocol for certificate issuance. PQC certificate issuance not available and no official PQC roadmap published as of Q1 2026. Migration to ML-DSA root certificates depends on browser and OS trust store acceptance. ISRG is monitoring NIST PQC standardization progress. Transport layer uses modern TLS but issued certificate signatures remain ECDSA P-384 or RSA.
product_brief: Free automated open CA. World's largest CA by volume with 160M+ active certs. No PQC roadmap published; migration is critical for the public web PKI ecosystem.

pqc_capability_description: Commercial password manager and enterprise secrets platform used by 100K+ businesses. Vault data encrypted with AES-256-GCM (quantum-resistant symmetric encryption). Authentication and vault key wrapping use SRP and RSA/ECC operations that are Shor-vulnerable. Enterprise offering (1Password Business) manages application secrets and credentials. No announced PQC roadmap as of Q1 2026. Classical key exchange layer requires PQC migration.
primary_platforms: Windows macOS iOS Android Linux Browser
product_brief: Password manager and enterprise secrets vault used by over 100K businesses. AES-256 vault data is quantum-safe; authentication and key exchange layer requires PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: Open-source password manager with enterprise and self-hosted deployment options. Vault data encrypted with AES-256-CBC using PBKDF2 key derivation (quantum-resistant symmetric encryption). RSA-4096 used for vault sharing between users — Shor-vulnerable. Bitwarden Secrets Manager (developer secrets product) also uses classical RSA key sharing. No announced PQC migration roadmap as of Q1 2026.
primary_platforms: Windows macOS iOS Android Linux Browser Self-hosted
product_brief: Open-source password manager with self-hosted option. Symmetric vault encryption is quantum-safe but RSA key sharing requires PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: Google's cloud email and productivity platform. Transport layer protected by ML-KEM hybrid TLS via BoringSSL on Google's servers — HNDL-resistant for email in transit since 2024. S/MIME end-to-end encryption uses classical RSA/ECDSA certificates with no PQC S/MIME certificate issuance available. Google Workspace Client-Side Encryption (CSE) uses classical ECDH key wrapping. Full PQC for message content depends on PKI ecosystem and S/MIME standards maturity.
product_brief: World's largest email platform. ML-KEM hybrid TLS protects email in transit via BoringSSL. S/MIME and CSE end-to-end encryption rely on classical PKI — pending PQC certificate ecosystem maturity.

pqc_capability_description: Cloud-based messaging platform with 900M+ users. Uses MTProto 2.0 protocol with AES-256, RSA-2048 for key exchange, and SHA-256 — all classical. End-to-end encrypted Secret Chats use a Diffie-Hellman key exchange that is Shor-vulnerable. No announced PQC roadmap as of Q1 2026. Regular cloud messages are server-encrypted (not E2E), adding key management exposure.
primary_platforms: iOS Android Windows macOS Linux Web
product_brief: Widely-deployed messaging platform. MTProto 2.0 uses classical RSA-2048 key exchange and AES-256. No PQC roadmap announced; Secret Chat Diffie-Hellman is Shor-vulnerable.

pqc_capability_description: End-to-end encrypted messaging with 2B+ users. Uses Signal Protocol (same as Signal app) with Curve25519, AES-256, and HMAC-SHA256 — classical operations that are Shor/Grover-vulnerable. Meta has not announced deployment of Signal's PQXDH protocol in WhatsApp. Transport layer uses TLS via Meta's infrastructure. No public PQC roadmap as of Q1 2026.
product_brief: World's largest messaging app. Uses Signal Protocol with Curve25519 (Shor-vulnerable). Signal's PQXDH has not been deployed in WhatsApp. No PQC roadmap published.

pqc_capability_description: Cloud-enabled platform for benchmarking enterprise VPN gateways and IPSec systems migrating to NIST PQC algorithms. Security Test module emulates tens of thousands of concurrent users with realistic application traffic while measuring PQC-specific KPIs: handshake latency, SA establishment time, re-keying overhead, key size impact. Supports hybrid scenarios (PQC initiator plus classical responder) to validate backward compatibility. Key finding: IKEv2 SA establishment increases from 38ms (classical) to 12626ms with some PQC configurations. Partnership with QNu Labs for QKD/QRNG/hybrid PQC test badging.
target_industries: Enterprise; Government; Telecommunications; Defense; Financial Services
product_brief: First cloud-enabled platform specifically designed to stress-test enterprise VPN gateways and IPSec systems migrating to NIST PQC algorithms. Emulates realistic application workloads while measuring latency, throughput, and re-keying overhead. Partnership with QNu Labs enables QKD and QRNG test badging. Critical for federal agencies and enterprises facing PQC migration mandates.

pqc_capability_description: Enterprise network protocol analyzer supporting 740+ primary protocols with SSL/TLS handshake monitoring, digital certificate validation (expiration tracking; outdated protocol detection), and network packet inspection. Threat Forensics module (2025) adds advanced retrospective analysis for NetSecOps: pattern analysis across cryptographic protocol usage, historical traffic replay for incident investigation. PQC algorithm detection capabilities are being added as NIST standards are finalized and deployed in enterprise environments.
target_industries: Enterprise; Financial Services; Healthcare; Government; Telecommunications
product_brief: Enterprise-grade network protocol analyzer with passive TLS/SSL monitoring and certificate validation across 740+ protocols. Threat Forensics module enables retroactive cryptographic protocol analysis for incident investigation. PQC detection capabilities in development as hybrid algorithms roll out in enterprise networks.

pqc_capability_description: Cloud-native network security test platform validating hybrid post-quantum TLS 1.3 handshakes using X25519+ML-KEM-768 across distributed physical and cloud environments. Version 7.0.2 (Oct 2025) supports hybrid key exchange simulation with Cloudflare endpoint. Stateful traffic generation at 1; 10; 100; 400 Gbps. Community Edition available free. Integrates with BreakingPoint System via ATI-2025-15 application for hybrid PQC encrypted traffic simulation in security appliance testing.
target_industries: Enterprise; Telecommunications; Cloud Infrastructure; Financial Services
product_brief: Cloud-native security test platform validating hybrid PQC TLS 1.3 at scale. Simulates realistic workloads (video; collaboration; enterprise apps) while measuring quantum-resistant handshake performance across physical and cloud environments. Community Edition available at no cost for lab testing.

pqc_capability_description: Device security testing platform (via Riscure acquisition) using Crypto 3 modular framework for PQC side-channel and fault injection testing. Dilithium TVLA data generator and Known Key Analysis module validate ML-DSA implementations against power; timing; and electromagnetic side-channel leakage. ML-KEM and SLH-DSA support in pre-release. Captures millions of power traces per second using PXI-based DS1050A testbench. Collaborated with NXP on ML-DSA countermeasure validation and PQShield on industry readiness. Supports FIPS 140-3 Level 4 compliance verification for semiconductor and device vendors.
target_industries: Semiconductors; Automotive; Aerospace; IoT; Embedded Systems; Government Defense
product_brief: Device security platform for validating PQC implementations against side-channel and fault injection attacks. Crypto 3 framework with Dilithium TVLA generator identifies power/EM leakage in ML-DSA hardware. Essential for semiconductor vendors preparing PQC products for CMVP certification.

pqc_capability_description: Passive high-performance cryptographic data detection system deploying on network taps and SPAN ports. Identifies all cryptographic algorithms; key types; and certificate details across 100+ IT and OT protocols in real time without packet injection. First EU company to achieve NIST CAVP certification for all three finalized PQC algorithms (ML-KEM/FIPS 203; ML-DSA/FIPS 204; SLH-DSA/FIPS 205) in June 2025. On path to FIPS 140-3 module certification. Integrates with COMPASS Analytics Platform for governance reporting. Recommended by NIST NCCoE for cryptographic discovery in PQC migration workflows. Secure boot; hardened OS; integrity checks; PQC-secured TLS 1.3 management communications.
fips_validated: Partial (NIST CAVP certified; FIPS 140-3 in progress)
target_industries: Critical Infrastructure; Defense; Financial Services; Energy; Government
product_brief: Passive network probe detecting all cryptographic algorithms across 100+ IT/OT protocols in real time. Only EU vendor certified by NIST CAVP for all three finalized PQC algorithms. Recommended by NIST NCCoE for PQC migration crypto asset inventories. Zero packet injection ensures production safety.

pqc_capability_description: High-performance open-source PQC readiness scanner written in Rust. Two scan modes: tls-scan (probes HTTPS/TLS endpoints for PQC algorithm support; TLS version; cipher suite; hybrid key exchange detection) and ssh-scan (probes SSH servers for PQC host key and key exchange support). Outputs JSON with HTML report generation. Binary releases for Linux; macOS; Windows. Database filtering for detecting specific PQC algorithm OIDs. Developed by Anvil Secure.
product_brief: Fast open-source Rust tool for scanning TLS and SSH endpoints to detect PQC algorithm support. Identifies hybrid key exchange; PQC OIDs; and quantum-vulnerable configurations. JSON/HTML output for integration into security workflows. Free alternative to commercial scanners for lab and pre-production environments.

pqc_support: Yes (detects ML-KEM; ML-DSA in TLS; SSH; QUIC)
pqc_capability_description: Open-source passive network analyzer detecting post-quantum cryptography in encrypted network traffic. Analyzes flow metadata (TLS/SSH/QUIC handshake features) without storing payload content; preserving privacy. Detects hybrid algorithm usage; quantum readiness posture; and classical-only connections requiring migration. Developed by CipherIQ. Provides organization-wide visibility into PQC adoption progress across network segments.
product_brief: Privacy-preserving passive network analyzer detecting PQC algorithm usage in TLS; SSH; and QUIC traffic. Analyzes flow metadata only (no payload storage). Provides organization-wide quantum readiness visibility without network disruption. Free open-source alternative to commercial passive probes.

pqc_support: Yes (ML-KEM; ML-DSA; all NIST PQC standards)
pqc_capability_description: Open-source PQC benchmarking and evaluation framework automating computational and network performance measurement for NIST PQC algorithms. Uses OpenSSL with OQS provider to run standardized TLS 1.3 performance tests across x86 and ARM architectures. Measures: handshake latency; key generation time; signing/verification overhead; memory footprint; energy consumption. Generates comparative analysis between classical; hybrid; and pure PQC configurations. Designed for academic research and enterprise pre-deployment evaluation.
product_brief: Automated PQC benchmarking framework measuring computational and TLS-layer performance for all NIST PQC algorithms across x86 and ARM. Generates baseline vs PQC comparison reports essential for hardware capacity planning and migration go/no-go decisions. Free and open source; peer-reviewed in academic literature.

pqc_support: Yes (ML-KEM; ML-DSA; SLH-DSA; FALCON; HQC; BIKE; NTRU; MAYO; CROSS; UOV)
pqc_capability_description: Free open-source TLS vulnerability auditing tool that analyzes cipher suites, certificates, and quantum readiness across domains. Part of Q-CORE platform with 90+ security modules including Q-AUTOPILOT autonomous SOC orchestrator and Q-CRA EU Cyber Resilience Act compliance dashboard. Supports broad PQC algorithm portfolio across tiered offerings.
product_brief: PQC-aware cryptographic discovery and compliance platform with free scanner tool. Covers TLS audit, cipher suite analysis, quantum readiness scoring, and EU CRA/NIS2 compliance mapping.

pqc_support: Yes (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, LMS, XMSS, Falcon)
pqc_capability_description: FIPS 140-3 certified cryptographic library from PQShield supporting all NIST-standardized PQC algorithms: ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205), plus hash-based LMS and XMSS and Falcon. OpenSSL integration via provider. CMVP certificate #4800 (Level 3); ACVP validated for ML-DSA and ML-KEM (A7693); additional ACVP validation (A3011) and CMVP module in process. Targets enterprise and government deployments.
product_brief: PQShield FIPS 140-3 L3 certified PQC library with OpenSSL provider integration. Full NIST PQC suite: ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, LMS, XMSS, Falcon. CMVP #4800 active; ACVP A7693 validated; additional validation in process.

pqc_capability_description: Embedded-optimized PQC cryptographic library from PQShield targeting ARM and RISC-V microcontrollers. Implements ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205), LMS, and XMSS. PSA Crypto API certified and integrates with Mbed TLS and the PSA Cryptography framework. CAVP algorithm validations complete (A3011); FIPS 140-3 CMVP module in process. Designed for resource-constrained IoT, automotive, and industrial embedded systems.
product_brief: PQShield embedded PQC library for ARM and RISC-V microcontrollers. Full NIST PQC suite with PSA Crypto API certification and Mbed TLS integration. CAVP validated (A3011); FIPS 140-3 CMVP in process. Optimized for constrained IoT and embedded deployments.

pqc_capability_description: Conformance testing harness for ML-KEM (FIPS 203) and ML-DSA (FIPS 204) cryptographic library implementations. Tests real-world vulnerabilities that evade both NIST Known Answer Tests and formal verification tools. ML-KEM: 78 tests across 6 categories (compression algorithms; NTT operations; coefficient bounds validation; decapsulation logic; serialization; sampling distributions). ML-DSA: 51 tests across 6 categories (norm checks; arithmetic operations; signing determinism; verification robustness; serialization; timing consistency). Uses a language-agnostic JSON line protocol enabling harness support for C; C++; Go; Java; Rust; and other languages. Ships with harnesses for 15 production implementations including CIRCL; liboqs; AWS-LC; Go stdlib; and Bouncy Castle. Created by Symbolic Software.
product_brief: Open-source conformance testing harness for ML-KEM and ML-DSA library implementations. Catches implementation bugs that evade NIST Known Answer Tests — tests 15 production implementations across 5 languages using a language-agnostic JSON protocol. 78 ML-KEM + 51 ML-DSA targeted tests covering NTT operations; serialization; coefficient bounds; timing consistency; and sampling distributions. Essential for cryptographic library vendors validating FIPS 203/204 implementation correctness before deployment.

pqc_capability_description: Serverless CIAM processing 100B+ authentications/month. PQC TLS transport (ML-KEM) being rolled out across AWS infrastructure including authentication endpoints. OIDC/OAuth token signing with PQC algorithms awaiting IETF JOSE/COSE PQC standard finalization. JWT/SAML/OIDC tokens rely on classical RSA/ECDSA signatures pending PQC migration.
product_brief: Serverless CIAM at massive scale. PQC TLS transport via AWS infrastructure rollout. OIDC/SAML token PQC signatures pending IETF standards.

pqc_support: Planned (PQC TLS and Token Signing via CNG/SymCrypt)
pqc_capability_description: Microsoft CIAM platform (successor to Azure AD B2C). PQC TLS transport available via Windows CNG ML-KEM in TLS 1.3. OIDC/SAML token signing with ML-DSA on roadmap per Microsoft quantum-safe strategy targeting 2029. Inherits FIPS 140-3 and FedRAMP High from Azure platform.
product_brief: Microsoft CIAM successor to Azure AD B2C. PQC TLS and token signing on roadmap via CNG/SymCrypt. Built on Entra platform with FIPS 140-3.

pqc_capability_description: Open-source CIAM platform supporting OIDC SAML and OAuth 2.0 with self-hosted and cloud deployment options. No PQC support or public quantum-safe roadmap. Self-hosted deployments require customer-managed TLS and JWT signing key migration to PQC algorithms. Classical RSA/ECDSA used for token signatures.
product_brief: Open-source CIAM with self-hosted option. No PQC support. Self-hosted customers must independently plan TLS and JWT signing PQC migration.

pqc_capability_description: Developer-first CIAM platform with drag-and-drop authentication flow builder. Supports OIDC SAML OAuth 2.0 passkeys and social login. No PQC support or public quantum-safe roadmap detected. SOC 2 Type II certified. Classical cryptography for all token signing and session management.
product_brief: Developer-first CIAM with visual flow builder. No PQC support. Enterprises using Descope need to track vendor PQC roadmap for token and session security.

pqc_capability_description: API-first authentication platform for passwordless login OTP OAuth and session management. No PQC support or public quantum-safe roadmap detected. SOC 2 Type II certified. Issues JWTs with classical RSA/ECDSA signatures. Session tokens and API keys use classical cryptography requiring future PQC migration.
product_brief: API-first authentication platform. No PQC support. JWT and session token cryptography requires PQC migration planning for enterprises.
